-- GENERATED by ./dev codemap — do not edit. Source of truth: the Haskell modules.
module Integration.Acs.Response

-- | ACS's success payload for an accepted email send. ACS is asynchronous;
--   <a>operationId</a> identifies the send for later delivery-status
--   polling. Decoded from the ACS wire field <tt>id</tt> (not
--   <tt>operationId</tt>).
data Response
Response :: Text -> Response
[operationId] :: Response -> Text
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Integration.Acs.Response.Response
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Integration.Acs.Response.Response
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Integration.Acs.Response.Response
instance GHC.Show.Show Integration.Acs.Response.Response
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Integration.Acs.Response.Response


-- | Request types for ACS transactional email integration.
--   
--   Provides the <a>Request</a> record and <a>Body</a> sum type that Jess
--   uses to configure email sends via Azure Communication Services.

module Integration.Acs.Request

-- | A complete ACS email send, produced by <a>send</a> and consumed by
--   <a>outbound</a>. <a>accessToken</a> is 'Redacted Text' so the Entra
--   Bearer token is never logged or serialized by accident.
data Request command
Request :: Text -> Sender -> Array Recipient -> Text -> Body -> Redacted Text -> (Response -> command) -> (Text -> command) -> Request command
[endpoint] :: Request command -> Text
[sender] :: Request command -> Sender
[to] :: Request command -> Array Recipient
[subject] :: Request command -> Text
[body] :: Request command -> Body
[accessToken] :: Request command -> Redacted Text
[onSuccess] :: Request command -> Response -> command
[onError] :: Request command -> Text -> command

-- | Mutually-exclusive email body. ACS rejects a request that sets both
--   <tt>html</tt> and <tt>plainText</tt>; the sum enforces the invariant
--   at compile time.
data Body
HtmlBody :: Text -> Body
TextBody :: Text -> Body

-- | A wire-format email address: optional display name + email string.
data Address
Address :: Maybe Text -> Text -> Address
[name] :: Address -> Maybe Text
[email] :: Address -> Text

-- | The sender address. A newtype over <a>Address</a> to prevent
--   accidental swap with <a>Recipient</a>. <a>Show</a> redacts the email
--   (no PII leak).
newtype Sender
Sender :: Address -> Sender

-- | A recipient address. A newtype over <a>Address</a> to prevent
--   accidental swap with <a>Sender</a>. <a>Show</a> redacts the email (no
--   PII leak).
newtype Recipient
Recipient :: Address -> Recipient

-- | Build a sender from just an email address (no display name).
--   
--   <pre>
--   Acs.sender "noreply@myapp.com"
--   </pre>
sender :: Text -> Sender

-- | Build a recipient from just an email address (no display name).
--   
--   <pre>
--   Acs.recipient "user@example.com"
--   </pre>
recipient :: Text -> Recipient

-- | Send one transactional email through an ACS Email resource. Reads the
--   Entra Bearer token from <tt>?config.acsAccessToken</tt>. The endpoint
--   must use <tt>https://</tt> — a non-https endpoint is reported through
--   the error callback without ever unwrapping the token.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Acs.send "<a>https://my-acs.communication.azure.com"</a>
--     (Acs.sender "noreply@myapp.com") (Acs.recipient info.email)
--     "Your order is confirmed" (Acs.HtmlBody "&lt;h1&gt;Thanks&lt;/h1&gt;")
--     (\r -&gt; EmailSent { operationId = r.operationId }) (\e -&gt; EmailFailed { reason = e })
--     |&gt; Integration.outbound
--   </pre>
send :: forall command config. (?config :: config, HasField "acsAccessToken" config (Redacted Text)) => Text -> Sender -> Recipient -> Text -> Body -> (Response -> command) -> (Text -> command) -> Request command
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Integration.Acs.Request.Address
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Integration.Acs.Request.Body
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Integration.Acs.Request.Recipient
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Integration.Acs.Request.Sender
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Integration.Acs.Request.Address
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Integration.Acs.Request.Body
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Integration.Acs.Request.Address
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Integration.Acs.Request.Body
instance GHC.Show.Show Integration.Acs.Request.Address
instance GHC.Show.Show Integration.Acs.Request.Body
instance GHC.Show.Show Integration.Acs.Request.Recipient
instance GHC.Show.Show Integration.Acs.Request.Sender
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Integration.Acs.Request.Address
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Integration.Acs.Request.Body


module Integration.Agent.Types

-- | Provider-agnostic AI agent request.
--   
--   The <tt>command</tt> type parameter is the domain command type emitted
--   on success or failure. Both <a>onError</a> and the internal dispatch
--   must produce the same <tt>command</tt>.
--   
--   NOTE: <a>Request</a> intentionally does NOT derive <a>Show</a>. Do not
--   add <a>Show</a> — <a>prompt</a> contains user data transmitted to
--   third-party AI providers. See ADR-0045 §12.
--   
--   When using multiple tools, <tt>command</tt> must be a sum type or
--   envelope type whose <tt>FromJSON</tt> instance handles all tool
--   argument shapes. See §8 for a complete example.
data Request command
Request :: Text -> Array CommandTool -> Text -> Config -> (Text -> command) -> Request command

-- | User prompt sent to the AI model
[prompt] :: Request command -> Text

-- | Available commands exposed as tools. Must be non-empty.
[tools] :: Request command -> Array CommandTool

-- | Model identifier (e.g., "anthropic/claude-3.5-sonnet")
[model] :: Request command -> Text

-- | Optional tuning parameters
[config] :: Request command -> Config

-- | Callback for AI failures, malformed responses, or network errors
[onError] :: Request command -> Text -> command

-- | Optional configuration for an Agent request.
--   
--   Use <a>defaultConfig</a> as a starting point:
--   
--   <pre>
--   Agent.defaultConfig
--     { systemPrompt = Just "You are a helpful shopping assistant."
--     , temperature  = Just 0.2
--     }
--   </pre>
data Config
Config :: Maybe Text -> Maybe Float -> Maybe Int -> {-# UNPACK #-}Int -> Config

-- | Optional system prompt. Nothing = no system message.
[systemPrompt] :: Config -> Maybe Text

-- | Sampling temperature (0.0-2.0). Lower values produce more
--   deterministic tool selection. Nothing = model default.
[temperature] :: Config -> Maybe Float

-- | Maximum tokens in the response. Nothing = model default.
[maxTokens] :: Config -> Maybe Int

-- | Request timeout in seconds. Default: 120.
[timeoutSeconds] :: Config -> {-# UNPACK #-}Int

-- | A tool extracted from a command type for use with AI agents.
--   
--   Build with <tt>commandTool</tt> to ensure the name, description, and
--   schema are consistent with the actual command type:
--   
--   <pre>
--   tools =
--     [ Agent.commandTool @AddItem
--     , Agent.commandTool @RemoveItem
--     ]
--   </pre>
--   
--   The description is pulled from the <tt>Documented</tt> typeclass
--   instance. If no <tt>Documented</tt> instance exists or description is
--   empty, the tool name is used as the description.
data CommandTool
CommandTool :: Text -> Text -> Value -> CommandTool

-- | Wire name derived from <tt>NameOf</tt> (e.g., <a>AddItem</a>)
[toolName] :: CommandTool -> Text

-- | Human-readable description from <tt>Documented</tt> instance
[toolDescription] :: CommandTool -> Text

-- | Pre-computed tool definition:
--   {"type":"function","function":{name,description,parameters}}. Built
--   once at <tt>commandTool</tt> call site to avoid per-request
--   allocation.
[toolDefinition] :: CommandTool -> Value

-- | Default agent configuration.
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>System prompt: None</li>
--   <li>Temperature: None (model default)</li>
--   <li>Max tokens: None (model default)</li>
--   <li>Timeout: 120 seconds</li>
--   </ul>
defaultConfig :: Config
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Integration.Agent.Types.CommandTool
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Integration.Agent.Types.Config
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Integration.Agent.Types.CommandTool
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Integration.Agent.Types.Config
instance GHC.Generics.Generic (Integration.Agent.Types.Request command)
instance GHC.Show.Show Integration.Agent.Types.CommandTool


module Integration.Agent

-- | Provider-agnostic AI agent request.
--   
--   The <tt>command</tt> type parameter is the domain command type emitted
--   on success or failure. Both <a>onError</a> and the internal dispatch
--   must produce the same <tt>command</tt>.
--   
--   NOTE: <a>Request</a> intentionally does NOT derive <a>Show</a>. Do not
--   add <a>Show</a> — <a>prompt</a> contains user data transmitted to
--   third-party AI providers. See ADR-0045 §12.
--   
--   When using multiple tools, <tt>command</tt> must be a sum type or
--   envelope type whose <tt>FromJSON</tt> instance handles all tool
--   argument shapes. See §8 for a complete example.
data Request command
Request :: Text -> Array CommandTool -> Text -> Config -> (Text -> command) -> Request command

-- | User prompt sent to the AI model
[prompt] :: Request command -> Text

-- | Available commands exposed as tools. Must be non-empty.
[tools] :: Request command -> Array CommandTool

-- | Model identifier (e.g., "anthropic/claude-3.5-sonnet")
[model] :: Request command -> Text

-- | Optional tuning parameters
[config] :: Request command -> Config

-- | Callback for AI failures, malformed responses, or network errors
[onError] :: Request command -> Text -> command

-- | Optional configuration for an Agent request.
--   
--   Use <a>defaultConfig</a> as a starting point:
--   
--   <pre>
--   Agent.defaultConfig
--     { systemPrompt = Just "You are a helpful shopping assistant."
--     , temperature  = Just 0.2
--     }
--   </pre>
data Config
Config :: Maybe Text -> Maybe Float -> Maybe Int -> {-# UNPACK #-}Int -> Config

-- | Optional system prompt. Nothing = no system message.
[systemPrompt] :: Config -> Maybe Text

-- | Sampling temperature (0.0-2.0). Lower values produce more
--   deterministic tool selection. Nothing = model default.
[temperature] :: Config -> Maybe Float

-- | Maximum tokens in the response. Nothing = model default.
[maxTokens] :: Config -> Maybe Int

-- | Request timeout in seconds. Default: 120.
[timeoutSeconds] :: Config -> {-# UNPACK #-}Int

-- | A tool extracted from a command type for use with AI agents.
--   
--   Build with <tt>commandTool</tt> to ensure the name, description, and
--   schema are consistent with the actual command type:
--   
--   <pre>
--   tools =
--     [ Agent.commandTool @AddItem
--     , Agent.commandTool @RemoveItem
--     ]
--   </pre>
--   
--   The description is pulled from the <tt>Documented</tt> typeclass
--   instance. If no <tt>Documented</tt> instance exists or description is
--   empty, the tool name is used as the description.
data CommandTool
CommandTool :: Text -> Text -> Value -> CommandTool

-- | Wire name derived from <tt>NameOf</tt> (e.g., <a>AddItem</a>)
[toolName] :: CommandTool -> Text

-- | Human-readable description from <tt>Documented</tt> instance
[toolDescription] :: CommandTool -> Text

-- | Pre-computed tool definition:
--   {"type":"function","function":{name,description,parameters}}. Built
--   once at <tt>commandTool</tt> call site to avoid per-request
--   allocation.
[toolDefinition] :: CommandTool -> Value

-- | Default agent configuration.
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>System prompt: None</li>
--   <li>Temperature: None (model default)</li>
--   <li>Max tokens: None (model default)</li>
--   <li>Timeout: 120 seconds</li>
--   </ul>
defaultConfig :: Config
commandTool :: forall command (name :: Symbol). (ToSchema command, Documented command, NameOf command ~ name, KnownSymbol name) => CommandTool
agent :: Text -> Array CommandTool -> Text -> (Text -> command) -> Request command


-- | # AI-Powered Audio Transcription Integration
--   
--   This module provides AI-powered audio transcription using multimodal
--   models (Gemini, GPT-4o) via the OpenRouter API.
--   
--   <h2>Two-Persona Model</h2>
--   
--   Following ADR-0008 and ADR-0041, this integration separates concerns:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><b>Jess (Integration User)</b>: Configures transcription with pure
--   records. No <tt>Task</tt>, no API calls, no base64 encoding
--   visible.</li>
--   <li><b>Nick (Integration Developer)</b>: Implements <tt>ToAction</tt>
--   with file retrieval, base64 encoding, and OpenRouter orchestration in
--   <a>Integration.Audio.Transcribe.Internal</a>.</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   <h2>Quick Start</h2>
--   
--   <pre>
--   import Integration qualified
--   import Integration.Audio.Transcribe qualified as AudioTranscribe
--   
--   meetingIntegrations :: Meeting -&gt; MeetingEvent -&gt; Integration.Outbound
--   meetingIntegrations meeting event = case event of
--     RecordingUploaded info -&gt; Integration.batch
--       [ Integration.outbound AudioTranscribe.Request
--           { fileRef = info.fileRef
--           , mimeType = "audio/mpeg"
--           , model = "google/gemini-2.0-flash"
--           , config = AudioTranscribe.defaultConfig
--           , onSuccess = \result -&gt; MeetingNotesReady
--               { meetingId = meeting.id
--               , transcript = result.text
--               }
--           , onError = \err -&gt; TranscriptionFailed
--               { meetingId = meeting.id
--               , error = err
--               }
--           }
--       ]
--     _ -&gt; Integration.none
--   </pre>
--   
--   <h2>Supported Audio Formats</h2>
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><tt>audio/wav</tt> — WAV (uncompressed)</li>
--   <li><tt>audio/mpeg</tt> — MP3 (compressed, most common)</li>
--   <li><tt>audio/mp4</tt> — M4A/AAC (compressed, Apple devices)</li>
--   <li><tt>audio/ogg</tt> — OGG/OGA (open-source, web)</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   <h2>Dispatcher Timeout Warning</h2>
--   
--   The default <a>timeoutSeconds</a> is 180 (3 minutes). The integration
--   dispatcher's <tt>eventProcessingTimeoutMs</tt> defaults to 30000 (30
--   seconds). __You MUST configure the dispatcher timeout to at least
--   180000 (or <a>Nothing</a> to disable)__, otherwise the dispatcher will
--   kill the worker before transcription completes:
--   
--   <pre>
--   Application.new
--     |&gt; Application.withDispatcherConfig Dispatcher.defaultConfig
--         { Dispatcher.eventProcessingTimeoutMs = Just 180000
--         }
--   </pre>
--   
--   <h2>Data Privacy Notice</h2>
--   
--   Audio file content is sent to third-party AI providers via OpenRouter.
--   Audio data may contain sensitive spoken content (PII, medical, legal).
--   Ensure appropriate data processing agreements are in place. See
--   ADR-0041 for security and privacy considerations.
--   
--   <h2>Recommended File Sizes</h2>
--   
--   Keep audio files under 25 MB and prefer compressed formats (MP3, M4A,
--   OGG) over uncompressed (WAV) for large recordings. For files exceeding
--   model input limits, see issue #457 (chunked transcription).
--   
--   <h2>Known Limitations (v1)</h2>
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>No streaming support</li>
--   <li>No confidence scoring (always Nothing)</li>
--   <li>No chunked transcription for large files (see #457)</li>
--   <li>Duration and language detection depend on model capabilities</li>
--   </ul>

module Integration.Audio.Transcribe

-- | The main request configuration record that Jess instantiates.
--   
--   The <tt>command</tt> type parameter is the domain command emitted by
--   <a>onSuccess</a> or <a>onError</a> callbacks.
--   
--   <h2>Fields</h2>
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><a>fileRef</a>: Reference to the uploaded audio file</li>
--   <li><a>mimeType</a>: MIME type of the audio file (e.g.,
--   <tt>"audio/wav"</tt>, <tt>"audio/mpeg"</tt>,
--   <tt>"audio/mp4"</tt>)</li>
--   <li><a>model</a>: AI model to use (e.g.,
--   <tt>"google/gemini-2.0-flash"</tt>)</li>
--   <li><a>config</a>: Transcription configuration (use
--   <a>defaultConfig</a>)</li>
--   <li><a>onSuccess</a>: Callback that receives result and returns a
--   domain command</li>
--   <li><a>onError</a>: Callback for error handling</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   <h2>Example</h2>
--   
--   <pre>
--   Integration.outbound AudioTranscribe.Request
--     { fileRef = e.uploadedFile
--     , mimeType = "audio/mpeg"
--     , model = "google/gemini-2.0-flash"
--     , config = AudioTranscribe.defaultConfig
--         { language = Just "en"
--         }
--     , onSuccess = \result -&gt; RecordTranscription
--         { text = result.text
--         , duration = result.duration
--         }
--     , onError = \err -&gt; TranscriptionFailed
--         { error = err
--         }
--     }
--   </pre>
data Request command
Request :: FileRef -> Text -> Text -> Config -> (TranscriptionResult -> command) -> (Text -> command) -> Request command

-- | Reference to the uploaded audio file
[fileRef] :: Request command -> FileRef

-- | MIME type of the audio file (e.g., "audio<i>wav", "audio</i>mpeg",
--   "audio/mp4")
[mimeType] :: Request command -> Text

-- | AI model to use (e.g., "google<i>gemini-2.0-flash",
--   "openai</i>gpt-4o-audio-preview")
[model] :: Request command -> Text

-- | Transcription configuration
[config] :: Request command -> Config

-- | Callback for successful transcription
[onSuccess] :: Request command -> TranscriptionResult -> command

-- | Callback for transcription errors
[onError] :: Request command -> Text -> command

-- | Configuration for audio transcription.
--   
--   Use <a>defaultConfig</a> for sensible defaults, then override specific
--   fields:
--   
--   <pre>
--   AudioTranscribe.defaultConfig
--     { language = Just "en"
--     , maxDurationSeconds = Just 300
--     }
--   </pre>
data Config
Config :: Maybe Text -> Maybe Int -> Maybe Text -> Int -> Config

-- | Language hint for the AI model (e.g., "en", "es", "de"). Improves
--   transcription accuracy for known languages. Nothing = auto-detect.
[language] :: Config -> Maybe Text

-- | Soft hint for maximum audio duration in seconds. The model is asked to
--   transcribe only this many seconds. Note: the full file is still
--   uploaded — this does NOT truncate the payload. Nothing = no limit
--   (process entire file). Non-positive values are ignored (treated as
--   Nothing). Useful for capping costs on long recordings.
[maxDurationSeconds] :: Config -> Maybe Int

-- | Override the default system prompt. Nothing = use the built-in
--   transcription prompt.
[systemPrompt] :: Config -> Maybe Text

-- | Timeout for the API call (default: 180). Audio transcription is
--   typically slower than OCR, so the default is higher than OCR's 120
--   seconds.
[timeoutSeconds] :: Config -> Int

-- | Result of audio transcription.
--   
--   <pre>
--   onSuccess = \result -&gt; RecordTranscription
--     { text = result.text
--     , duration = result.duration
--     , language = result.language
--     }
--   </pre>
data TranscriptionResult
TranscriptionResult :: Text -> Maybe Float -> Maybe Float -> Maybe Text -> TranscriptionResult

-- | Transcribed text from the audio
[text] :: TranscriptionResult -> Text

-- | Duration of the audio in seconds (if reported by the model). Float is
--   Double-precision in NeoHaskell.
[duration] :: TranscriptionResult -> Maybe Float

-- | Confidence score (always Nothing for v1). Reserved for future model
--   support.
[confidence] :: TranscriptionResult -> Maybe Float

-- | Detected language code (e.g., "en", "es"). Populated when the model
--   reports the detected language.
[language] :: TranscriptionResult -> Maybe Text

-- | Default configuration for audio transcription.
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>Language: None (auto-detect)</li>
--   <li>Max duration: None (process entire file)</li>
--   <li>System prompt: None (use default)</li>
--   <li>Timeout: 180 seconds</li>
--   </ul>
defaultConfig :: Config
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Integration.Audio.Transcribe.Config
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Integration.Audio.Transcribe.TranscriptionResult
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Integration.Audio.Transcribe.Config
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Integration.Audio.Transcribe.TranscriptionResult
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Integration.Audio.Transcribe.Config
instance GHC.Generics.Generic (Integration.Audio.Transcribe.Request command)
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Integration.Audio.Transcribe.TranscriptionResult
instance GHC.Show.Show Integration.Audio.Transcribe.Config
instance GHC.Show.Show Integration.Audio.Transcribe.TranscriptionResult
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Integration.Audio.Transcribe.Config
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Integration.Audio.Transcribe.TranscriptionResult


-- | Response types for Brevo transactional email integration.
--   
--   Provides the <a>Response</a> record decoded from Brevo's HTTP 201
--   response.

module Integration.Brevo.Response

-- | Brevo's success response for a single email send.
data Response
Response :: Text -> Response
[messageId] :: Response -> Text
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Integration.Brevo.Response.Response
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Integration.Brevo.Response.Response
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Integration.Brevo.Response.Response
instance GHC.Show.Show Integration.Brevo.Response.Response
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Integration.Brevo.Response.Response


-- | Request types for Brevo transactional email integration.
--   
--   Provides the <a>Request</a> record and <a>Body</a> sum type that Jess
--   uses to configure email sends.

module Integration.Brevo.Request

-- | Complete Brevo SMTP email request. The <a>apiKey</a> field is
--   'Redacted Text' to prevent accidental logging or serialization of the
--   secret.
data Request command
Request :: Sender -> Array Recipient -> Text -> Body -> Array Recipient -> Array Recipient -> Maybe Recipient -> Array Text -> Redacted Text -> (Response -> command) -> (Text -> command) -> Request command
[sender] :: Request command -> Sender
[to] :: Request command -> Array Recipient
[subject] :: Request command -> Text
[body] :: Request command -> Body
[cc] :: Request command -> Array Recipient
[bcc] :: Request command -> Array Recipient
[replyTo] :: Request command -> Maybe Recipient
[tags] :: Request command -> Array Text
[apiKey] :: Request command -> Redacted Text
[onSuccess] :: Request command -> Response -> command
[onError] :: Request command -> Text -> command

-- | Mutually-exclusive email body type. Brevo refuses requests that
--   combine htmlContent, textContent, and templateId; this sum type
--   enforces the invariant at compile time.
data Body
HtmlBody :: Text -> Body
TextBody :: Text -> Body
Template :: Int -> Map Text Text -> Body
[templateId] :: Body -> Int
[params] :: Body -> Map Text Text

-- | A wire-format email address: optional display name + email string.
data Address
Address :: Maybe Text -> Text -> Address
[name] :: Address -> Maybe Text
[email] :: Address -> Text

-- | The sender address of a transactional email. A newtype over
--   <a>Address</a> to prevent accidental swap with <a>Recipient</a>.
newtype Sender
Sender :: Address -> Sender

-- | A recipient address (one of: to, cc, bcc, replyTo). A newtype over
--   <a>Address</a> to prevent accidental swap with <a>Sender</a>.
newtype Recipient
Recipient :: Address -> Recipient

-- | Build a sender from just an email address (no display name).
sender :: Text -> Sender

-- | Build a recipient from just an email address (no display name).
recipient :: Text -> Recipient

-- | Send a transactional email with a single sender and a single
--   recipient.
--   
--   Reads the API key from the user's project config via the implicit
--   ?config parameter.
send :: forall command config. (?config :: config, HasField "brevoApiKey" config (Redacted Text)) => Sender -> Recipient -> Text -> Body -> (Response -> command) -> (Text -> command) -> Request command
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Integration.Brevo.Request.Address
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Integration.Brevo.Request.Body
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Integration.Brevo.Request.Recipient
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Integration.Brevo.Request.Sender
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Integration.Brevo.Request.Address
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Integration.Brevo.Request.Body
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Integration.Brevo.Request.Address
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Integration.Brevo.Request.Body
instance GHC.Show.Show Integration.Brevo.Request.Address
instance GHC.Show.Show Integration.Brevo.Request.Body
instance GHC.Show.Show Integration.Brevo.Request.Recipient
instance GHC.Show.Show Integration.Brevo.Request.Sender
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Integration.Brevo.Request.Address
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Integration.Brevo.Request.Body
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Integration.Brevo.Request.Recipient
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Integration.Brevo.Request.Sender


module Integration.Brevo

-- | Complete Brevo SMTP email request. The <a>apiKey</a> field is
--   'Redacted Text' to prevent accidental logging or serialization of the
--   secret.
data Request command
Request :: Sender -> Array Recipient -> Text -> Body -> Array Recipient -> Array Recipient -> Maybe Recipient -> Array Text -> Redacted Text -> (Response -> command) -> (Text -> command) -> Request command
[sender] :: Request command -> Sender
[to] :: Request command -> Array Recipient
[subject] :: Request command -> Text
[body] :: Request command -> Body
[cc] :: Request command -> Array Recipient
[bcc] :: Request command -> Array Recipient
[replyTo] :: Request command -> Maybe Recipient
[tags] :: Request command -> Array Text
[apiKey] :: Request command -> Redacted Text
[onSuccess] :: Request command -> Response -> command
[onError] :: Request command -> Text -> command

-- | Mutually-exclusive email body type. Brevo refuses requests that
--   combine htmlContent, textContent, and templateId; this sum type
--   enforces the invariant at compile time.
data Body
HtmlBody :: Text -> Body
TextBody :: Text -> Body
Template :: Int -> Map Text Text -> Body
[templateId] :: Body -> Int
[params] :: Body -> Map Text Text

-- | A wire-format email address: optional display name + email string.
data Address
Address :: Maybe Text -> Text -> Address
[name] :: Address -> Maybe Text
[email] :: Address -> Text

-- | The sender address of a transactional email. A newtype over
--   <a>Address</a> to prevent accidental swap with <a>Recipient</a>.
newtype Sender
Sender :: Address -> Sender

-- | A recipient address (one of: to, cc, bcc, replyTo). A newtype over
--   <a>Address</a> to prevent accidental swap with <a>Sender</a>.
newtype Recipient
Recipient :: Address -> Recipient

-- | Send a transactional email with a single sender and a single
--   recipient.
--   
--   Reads the API key from the user's project config via the implicit
--   ?config parameter.
send :: forall command config. (?config :: config, HasField "brevoApiKey" config (Redacted Text)) => Sender -> Recipient -> Text -> Body -> (Response -> command) -> (Text -> command) -> Request command

-- | Build a sender from just an email address (no display name).
sender :: Text -> Sender

-- | Build a recipient from just an email address (no display name).
recipient :: Text -> Recipient

-- | Brevo's success response for a single email send.
data Response
Response :: Text -> Response
[messageId] :: Response -> Text


-- | Authentication configuration for HTTP outbound integrations.
--   
--   This module provides authentication patterns for external APIs. All
--   values support environment variable expansion using
--   <tt>${VAR_NAME}</tt> syntax.
--   
--   <h2>Security</h2>
--   
--   Never hardcode secrets in source code. Always use environment
--   variables:
--   
--   <pre>
--   -- Good: Secret from environment
--   auth = Http.Bearer "${API_TOKEN}"
--   
--   -- Bad: Hardcoded secret
--   auth = Http.Bearer "sk_live_abc123"
--   </pre>

module Integration.Http.Auth

-- | Authentication configuration with environment variable expansion.
--   
--   Values like <tt>"${API_KEY}"</tt> are expanded from environment at
--   runtime. Missing variables result in <a>AuthenticationError</a>.
--   
--   <h2>Supported Patterns</h2>
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><a>NoAuth</a>: No authentication (e.g., public APIs, webhooks with
--   auth in URL)</li>
--   <li><a>Bearer</a>: OAuth2/JWT Bearer token in Authorization
--   header</li>
--   <li><a>Basic</a>: HTTP Basic auth with username and password</li>
--   <li><a>ApiKey</a>: Custom header with API key value</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   <h2>Examples</h2>
--   
--   <pre>
--   -- Bearer token (OAuth2, JWT)
--   auth = Http.Bearer "${GITHUB_TOKEN}"
--   -- Sends: Authorization: Bearer ghp_xxx...
--   
--   -- Basic auth
--   auth = Http.Basic "${STRIPE_KEY}" ""
--   -- Sends: Authorization: Basic base64(key:)
--   
--   -- API key in custom header
--   auth = Http.ApiKey "X-Api-Key" "${SHIPPO_API_KEY}"
--   -- Sends: X-Api-Key: shippo_xxx...
--   
--   -- No auth (webhook with auth in URL)
--   auth = Http.NoAuth
--   </pre>
data Auth

-- | No authentication required
NoAuth :: Auth

-- | Bearer token authentication
--   
--   Produces: <tt>Authorization: Bearer &lt;token&gt;</tt>
Bearer :: Text -> Auth

-- | HTTP Basic authentication
--   
--   Produces: <tt>Authorization: Basic base64(user:pass)</tt>
Basic :: Text -> Text -> Auth
[username] :: Auth -> Text
[password] :: Auth -> Text

-- | Custom API key header
--   
--   Produces: <tt>&lt;headerName&gt;: &lt;headerValue&gt;</tt>
ApiKey :: Text -> Text -> Auth
[headerName] :: Auth -> Text
[headerValue] :: Auth -> Text
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Integration.Http.Auth.Auth
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Integration.Http.Auth.Auth
instance GHC.Show.Show Integration.Http.Auth.Auth


-- | HTTP Response type for outbound integrations.
--   
--   The <a>Response</a> type is passed to <tt>onSuccess</tt> callbacks,
--   allowing Jess to extract data from API responses.

module Integration.Http.Response

-- | Response data extracted from HTTP response.
--   
--   Used in <tt>onSuccess</tt> callbacks to access response data.
--   
--   <pre>
--   onSuccess = \response -&gt;
--     NotifyCustomer
--       { trackingUrl = response.body
--           |&gt; Json.get "tracking_url"
--           |&gt; Maybe.withDefault ""
--       }
--   </pre>
--   
--   You can also check the status code for conditional logic:
--   
--   <pre>
--   onSuccess = \response -&gt;
--     if response.statusCode == 304
--       then ResourceNotModified
--       else ResourceUpdated { etag = findHeader <a>ETag</a> response.headers }
--   </pre>
data Response
Response :: Int -> Value -> Array (Text, Text) -> Response

-- | HTTP status code (e.g., 200, 201, 404)
[statusCode] :: Response -> Int

-- | Parsed JSON body (or Json.Null if not JSON)
[body] :: Response -> Value

-- | Response headers as key-value pairs
[headers] :: Response -> Array (Text, Text)
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Integration.Http.Response.Response
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Integration.Http.Response.Response
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Integration.Http.Response.Response
instance GHC.Show.Show Integration.Http.Response.Response
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Integration.Http.Response.Response


-- | Retry configuration for HTTP outbound integrations.
--   
--   This module provides retry policies with exponential backoff for
--   handling transient failures in external API calls.
--   
--   <h2>Retry Strategy</h2>
--   
--   By default, requests are retried up to 3 times with exponential
--   backoff:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>Attempt 1: immediate</li>
--   <li>Attempt 2: wait ~1 second</li>
--   <li>Attempt 3: wait ~2 seconds</li>
--   <li>Attempt 4: fail</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   Jitter (0-25% of delay) is added to prevent thundering herd.
--   
--   <h2>Retryable Errors</h2>
--   
--   Only transient errors are retried by default:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>429 Too Many Requests</li>
--   <li>500 Internal Server Error</li>
--   <li>502 Bad Gateway</li>
--   <li>503 Service Unavailable</li>
--   <li>504 Gateway Timeout</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   Client errors (4xx except 429) are NOT retried as they indicate a
--   problem with the request itself.

module Integration.Http.Retry

-- | Retry configuration for transient failures.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Http.Request
--     { ...
--     , retry = Http.Retry
--         { maxAttempts = 5
--         , initialDelayMs = 500
--         , maxDelayMs = 10000
--         , retryableStatuses = [429, 500, 502, 503, 504]
--         }
--     }
--   </pre>
data Retry
Retry :: Int -> Int -> Int -> Array Int -> Retry

-- | Maximum number of attempts (including initial request)
--   
--   Default: 3 (1 initial + 2 retries)
[maxAttempts] :: Retry -> Int

-- | Initial delay in milliseconds before first retry
--   
--   Default: 1000 (1 second)
[initialDelayMs] :: Retry -> Int

-- | Maximum delay in milliseconds (caps exponential growth)
--   
--   Default: 30000 (30 seconds)
[maxDelayMs] :: Retry -> Int

-- | HTTP status codes that trigger retry
--   
--   Default: [429, 500, 502, 503, 504]
[retryableStatuses] :: Retry -> Array Int

-- | Default retry configuration.
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>3 attempts (1 initial + 2 retries)</li>
--   <li>1 second initial delay</li>
--   <li>30 second max delay</li>
--   <li>Retries on: 429, 500, 502, 503, 504</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   <pre>
--   Http.Request
--     { ...
--     , retry = Http.defaultRetry
--     }
--   </pre>
defaultRetry :: Retry

-- | No retries - fail immediately on any error.
--   
--   Use for fire-and-forget requests where retries don't make sense:
--   
--   <pre>
--   -- Slack webhook - fire and forget
--   Http.Request
--     { ...
--     , retry = Http.noRetry
--     }
--   </pre>
noRetry :: Retry

-- | Custom retry count with default backoff settings.
--   
--   <pre>
--   -- 5 attempts with default delays
--   Http.Request
--     { ...
--     , retry = Http.withRetries 5
--     }
--   </pre>
withRetries :: Int -> Retry
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Integration.Http.Retry.Retry
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Integration.Http.Retry.Retry
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Integration.Http.Retry.Retry
instance GHC.Show.Show Integration.Http.Retry.Retry
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Integration.Http.Retry.Retry


-- | HTTP Request types for outbound integrations.
--   
--   This module defines the <a>Request</a> record that Jess configures to
--   make HTTP requests to external APIs.
--   
--   <h2>Example</h2>
--   
--   <pre>
--   Integration.outbound Http.Request
--     { method = Http.POST
--     , url = "<a>https://api.example.com/orders"</a>
--     , headers = []
--     , body = Http.json orderData
--     , onSuccess = \response -&gt; ConfirmOrder { ... }
--     , onError = Nothing
--     , auth = Http.Bearer "${API_TOKEN}"
--     , retry = Http.defaultRetry
--     , timeoutSeconds = 30
--     }
--   </pre>

module Integration.Http.Request

-- | The main request configuration record that Jess instantiates.
--   
--   The <tt>command</tt> type parameter is the domain command emitted by
--   <a>onSuccess</a> or <a>onError</a> callbacks.
--   
--   <h2>Fields</h2>
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><a>method</a>: HTTP method (GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE)</li>
--   <li><a>url</a>: Full URL including scheme, host, path. Supports
--   <tt>${VAR}</tt> expansion.</li>
--   <li><a>headers</a>: Additional headers as key-value pairs. Supports
--   <tt>${VAR}</tt> expansion.</li>
--   <li><a>body</a>: Request body (use <a>json</a>, <a>form</a>,
--   <a>raw</a>, or <a>noBody</a>)</li>
--   <li><a>onSuccess</a>: Callback that receives response and returns a
--   domain command</li>
--   <li><a>onError</a>: Optional callback for error handling (if Nothing,
--   integration fails)</li>
--   <li><a>auth</a>: Authentication configuration (see
--   <a>Integration.Http.Auth</a>)</li>
--   <li><a>retry</a>: Retry configuration (see
--   <a>Integration.Http.Retry</a>)</li>
--   <li><a>timeoutSeconds</a>: Request timeout in seconds</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   <h2>Environment Variable Expansion</h2>
--   
--   URL, headers, and auth values support <tt>${VAR_NAME}</tt> patterns
--   that are expanded from environment variables at runtime:
--   
--   <pre>
--   url = "<a>https://api.example.com/${API_VERSION}/orders"</a>
--   auth = Http.Bearer "${API_TOKEN}"
--   </pre>
data Request command
Request :: Method -> Text -> Array (Text, Text) -> Body -> (Response -> command) -> Maybe (Text -> command) -> Auth -> Retry -> Int -> Request command
[method] :: Request command -> Method
[url] :: Request command -> Text
[headers] :: Request command -> Array (Text, Text)
[body] :: Request command -> Body
[onSuccess] :: Request command -> Response -> command
[onError] :: Request command -> Maybe (Text -> command)
[auth] :: Request command -> Auth
[retry] :: Request command -> Retry
[timeoutSeconds] :: Request command -> Int

-- | HTTP methods supported for outbound requests.
--   
--   All methods are fully supported:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><a>GET</a> - Retrieve a resource</li>
--   <li><a>POST</a> - Create a new resource</li>
--   <li><a>PUT</a> - Replace an entire resource</li>
--   <li><a>PATCH</a> - Partially update a resource</li>
--   <li><a>DELETE</a> - Remove a resource</li>
--   </ul>
data Method
GET :: Method
POST :: Method
PUT :: Method
PATCH :: Method
DELETE :: Method

-- | Request body configuration.
--   
--   Use the helper functions <a>json</a>, <a>form</a>, <a>raw</a>, and
--   <a>noBody</a> to construct body values.
data Body

-- | JSON body from any ToJSON value
JsonBody :: Value -> Body

-- | Form-urlencoded body
FormBody :: Array (Text, Text) -> Body

-- | Raw body with custom content type
RawBody :: Text -> Text -> Body
[contentType] :: Body -> Text
[content] :: Body -> Text

-- | No request body
NoBody :: Body

-- | Create a JSON body from any ToJSON value.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Http.json { name = <a>Order</a>, items = [1, 2, 3] }
--   </pre>
json :: ToJSON a => a -> Body

-- | Create a form-urlencoded body.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Http.form
--     [ ("grant_type", "authorization_code")
--     , ("code", authCode)
--     ]
--   </pre>
form :: Array (Text, Text) -> Body

-- | Create a raw body with custom content type.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Http.raw "application<i>xml" "<a>order</a><a>id</a>123&lt;</i>id&gt;<a>/order</a>"
--   </pre>
raw :: Text -> Text -> Body

-- | No request body.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Http.Request { body = Http.noBody, ... }
--   </pre>
noBody :: Body
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Integration.Http.Request.Body
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Integration.Http.Request.Method
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Integration.Http.Request.Method
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Integration.Http.Request.Body
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Integration.Http.Request.Method
instance GHC.Generics.Generic (Integration.Http.Request.Request command)
instance GHC.Show.Show Integration.Http.Request.Body
instance GHC.Show.Show Integration.Http.Request.Method
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Integration.Http.Request.Method


-- | Internal implementation for HTTP outbound integration.
--   
--   This module contains Nick's code - the <tt>ToAction</tt> instance,
--   retry logic, environment variable expansion, and authentication header
--   building.
--   
--   <b>This module is not exported to Jess.</b>

module Integration.Http.Internal

-- | Expand environment variables in text.
--   
--   Patterns like "${VAR_NAME}" are replaced with environment values.
--   Missing variables throw <a>AuthenticationError</a>.
--   
--   <pre>
--   expandEnvVars "<a>https://api.example.com/${API_VERSION}/orders"</a>
--   -- With API_VERSION=v2: "<a>https://api.example.com/v2/orders"</a>
--   </pre>
expandEnvVars :: Text -> Task IntegrationError Text

-- | Build authentication header from Auth config.
--   
--   All values are expanded for environment variables before use.
buildAuthHeader :: Auth -> Task IntegrationError (Maybe (Text, Text))

-- | Calculate backoff delay with exponential increase and jitter.
--   
--   Formula: min(initialDelayMs * 2^(attempt-1), maxDelayMs) + jitter
--   Jitter: random value in [0, delay/4]
calculateBackoff :: Retry -> Int -> Task IntegrationError Int
instance (Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON command, GHC.TypeLits.KnownSymbol (Service.Command.Core.NameOf command)) => Integration.ToAction (Integration.Http.Request.Request command)


-- | # HTTP Outbound Integration
--   
--   This module provides HTTP outbound integration for NeoHaskell,
--   allowing Jess to make HTTP requests to external APIs using pure record
--   configuration.
--   
--   <h2>Two-Persona Model</h2>
--   
--   Following ADR-0008 and ADR-0015, this integration separates concerns:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>**Jess (Integration User)**: Configures requests with pure
--   records. No <tt>Task</tt>, no <tt>Http.Client</tt>, no retry logic
--   visible.</li>
--   <li>**Nick (Integration Developer)**: Implements <tt>ToAction</tt>
--   with retries, authentication, and error handling in
--   <a>Integration.Http.Internal</a>.</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   <h2>Quick Start</h2>
--   
--   <pre>
--   import Integration qualified
--   import Integration.Http qualified as Http
--   
--   orderIntegrations :: Order -&gt; OrderEvent -&gt; Integration.Outbound
--   orderIntegrations order event = case event of
--     OrderShipped info -&gt; Integration.batch
--       [ Integration.outbound Http.Request
--           { method = Http.POST
--           , url = "<a>https://api.shippo.com/v1/shipments"</a>
--           , headers = []
--           , body = Http.json
--               { tracking_number = info.trackingNumber
--               , carrier = info.carrier
--               }
--           , onSuccess = \response -&gt; NotifyCustomer
--               { orderId = order.id
--               , trackingUrl = response.body
--                   |&gt; Json.get "tracking_url"
--                   |&gt; Maybe.withDefault ""
--               }
--           , onError = Just (\err -&gt; LogShippingError
--               { orderId = order.id
--               , error = err
--               })
--           , auth = Http.ApiKey "X-Api-Key" "${SHIPPO_API_KEY}"
--           , retry = Http.defaultRetry
--           , timeoutSeconds = 30
--           }
--       ]
--     _ -&gt; Integration.none
--   </pre>
--   
--   <h2>Environment Variables</h2>
--   
--   Secrets should never be hardcoded. Use <tt>${VAR_NAME}</tt> patterns:
--   
--   <pre>
--   auth = Http.Bearer "${API_TOKEN}"
--   url = "<a>https://api.example.com/${API_VERSION}/orders"</a>
--   </pre>
--   
--   Missing variables result in <a>AuthenticationError</a>.
--   
--   <h2>Retry Behavior</h2>
--   
--   By default, requests are retried on transient errors (5xx, 429) with
--   exponential backoff. Use <a>defaultRetry</a>, <a>noRetry</a>, or
--   <a>withRetries</a> to configure.
--   
--   <h2>Known Limitations (v1)</h2>
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>PUT, PATCH, DELETE methods return "unsupported" error</li>
--   <li>Response status codes and headers are placeholders</li>
--   <li>No streaming support</li>
--   <li>No OAuth2 token refresh</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   These limitations are documented in ADR-0015 and will be addressed in
--   future versions.

module Integration.Http

-- | The main request configuration record that Jess instantiates.
--   
--   The <tt>command</tt> type parameter is the domain command emitted by
--   <a>onSuccess</a> or <a>onError</a> callbacks.
--   
--   <h2>Fields</h2>
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><a>method</a>: HTTP method (GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE)</li>
--   <li><a>url</a>: Full URL including scheme, host, path. Supports
--   <tt>${VAR}</tt> expansion.</li>
--   <li><a>headers</a>: Additional headers as key-value pairs. Supports
--   <tt>${VAR}</tt> expansion.</li>
--   <li><a>body</a>: Request body (use <a>json</a>, <a>form</a>,
--   <a>raw</a>, or <a>noBody</a>)</li>
--   <li><a>onSuccess</a>: Callback that receives response and returns a
--   domain command</li>
--   <li><a>onError</a>: Optional callback for error handling (if Nothing,
--   integration fails)</li>
--   <li><a>auth</a>: Authentication configuration (see
--   <a>Integration.Http.Auth</a>)</li>
--   <li><a>retry</a>: Retry configuration (see
--   <a>Integration.Http.Retry</a>)</li>
--   <li><a>timeoutSeconds</a>: Request timeout in seconds</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   <h2>Environment Variable Expansion</h2>
--   
--   URL, headers, and auth values support <tt>${VAR_NAME}</tt> patterns
--   that are expanded from environment variables at runtime:
--   
--   <pre>
--   url = "<a>https://api.example.com/${API_VERSION}/orders"</a>
--   auth = Http.Bearer "${API_TOKEN}"
--   </pre>
data Request command
Request :: Method -> Text -> Array (Text, Text) -> Body -> (Response -> command) -> Maybe (Text -> command) -> Auth -> Retry -> Int -> Request command
[method] :: Request command -> Method
[url] :: Request command -> Text
[headers] :: Request command -> Array (Text, Text)
[body] :: Request command -> Body
[onSuccess] :: Request command -> Response -> command
[onError] :: Request command -> Maybe (Text -> command)
[auth] :: Request command -> Auth
[retry] :: Request command -> Retry
[timeoutSeconds] :: Request command -> Int

-- | HTTP methods supported for outbound requests.
--   
--   All methods are fully supported:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><a>GET</a> - Retrieve a resource</li>
--   <li><a>POST</a> - Create a new resource</li>
--   <li><a>PUT</a> - Replace an entire resource</li>
--   <li><a>PATCH</a> - Partially update a resource</li>
--   <li><a>DELETE</a> - Remove a resource</li>
--   </ul>
data Method
GET :: Method
POST :: Method
PUT :: Method
PATCH :: Method
DELETE :: Method

-- | Request body configuration.
--   
--   Use the helper functions <a>json</a>, <a>form</a>, <a>raw</a>, and
--   <a>noBody</a> to construct body values.
data Body

-- | JSON body from any ToJSON value
JsonBody :: Value -> Body

-- | Form-urlencoded body
FormBody :: Array (Text, Text) -> Body

-- | Raw body with custom content type
RawBody :: Text -> Text -> Body
[contentType] :: Body -> Text
[content] :: Body -> Text

-- | No request body
NoBody :: Body

-- | Authentication configuration with environment variable expansion.
--   
--   Values like <tt>"${API_KEY}"</tt> are expanded from environment at
--   runtime. Missing variables result in <a>AuthenticationError</a>.
--   
--   <h2>Supported Patterns</h2>
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><a>NoAuth</a>: No authentication (e.g., public APIs, webhooks with
--   auth in URL)</li>
--   <li><a>Bearer</a>: OAuth2/JWT Bearer token in Authorization
--   header</li>
--   <li><a>Basic</a>: HTTP Basic auth with username and password</li>
--   <li><a>ApiKey</a>: Custom header with API key value</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   <h2>Examples</h2>
--   
--   <pre>
--   -- Bearer token (OAuth2, JWT)
--   auth = Http.Bearer "${GITHUB_TOKEN}"
--   -- Sends: Authorization: Bearer ghp_xxx...
--   
--   -- Basic auth
--   auth = Http.Basic "${STRIPE_KEY}" ""
--   -- Sends: Authorization: Basic base64(key:)
--   
--   -- API key in custom header
--   auth = Http.ApiKey "X-Api-Key" "${SHIPPO_API_KEY}"
--   -- Sends: X-Api-Key: shippo_xxx...
--   
--   -- No auth (webhook with auth in URL)
--   auth = Http.NoAuth
--   </pre>
data Auth

-- | No authentication required
NoAuth :: Auth

-- | Bearer token authentication
--   
--   Produces: <tt>Authorization: Bearer &lt;token&gt;</tt>
Bearer :: Text -> Auth

-- | HTTP Basic authentication
--   
--   Produces: <tt>Authorization: Basic base64(user:pass)</tt>
Basic :: Text -> Text -> Auth
[username] :: Auth -> Text
[password] :: Auth -> Text

-- | Custom API key header
--   
--   Produces: <tt>&lt;headerName&gt;: &lt;headerValue&gt;</tt>
ApiKey :: Text -> Text -> Auth
[headerName] :: Auth -> Text
[headerValue] :: Auth -> Text

-- | Retry configuration for transient failures.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Http.Request
--     { ...
--     , retry = Http.Retry
--         { maxAttempts = 5
--         , initialDelayMs = 500
--         , maxDelayMs = 10000
--         , retryableStatuses = [429, 500, 502, 503, 504]
--         }
--     }
--   </pre>
data Retry
Retry :: Int -> Int -> Int -> Array Int -> Retry

-- | Maximum number of attempts (including initial request)
--   
--   Default: 3 (1 initial + 2 retries)
[maxAttempts] :: Retry -> Int

-- | Initial delay in milliseconds before first retry
--   
--   Default: 1000 (1 second)
[initialDelayMs] :: Retry -> Int

-- | Maximum delay in milliseconds (caps exponential growth)
--   
--   Default: 30000 (30 seconds)
[maxDelayMs] :: Retry -> Int

-- | HTTP status codes that trigger retry
--   
--   Default: [429, 500, 502, 503, 504]
[retryableStatuses] :: Retry -> Array Int

-- | Response data extracted from HTTP response.
--   
--   Used in <tt>onSuccess</tt> callbacks to access response data.
--   
--   <pre>
--   onSuccess = \response -&gt;
--     NotifyCustomer
--       { trackingUrl = response.body
--           |&gt; Json.get "tracking_url"
--           |&gt; Maybe.withDefault ""
--       }
--   </pre>
--   
--   You can also check the status code for conditional logic:
--   
--   <pre>
--   onSuccess = \response -&gt;
--     if response.statusCode == 304
--       then ResourceNotModified
--       else ResourceUpdated { etag = findHeader <a>ETag</a> response.headers }
--   </pre>
data Response
Response :: Int -> Value -> Array (Text, Text) -> Response

-- | HTTP status code (e.g., 200, 201, 404)
[statusCode] :: Response -> Int

-- | Parsed JSON body (or Json.Null if not JSON)
[body] :: Response -> Value

-- | Response headers as key-value pairs
[headers] :: Response -> Array (Text, Text)

-- | Create a JSON body from any ToJSON value.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Http.json { name = <a>Order</a>, items = [1, 2, 3] }
--   </pre>
json :: ToJSON a => a -> Body

-- | Create a form-urlencoded body.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Http.form
--     [ ("grant_type", "authorization_code")
--     , ("code", authCode)
--     ]
--   </pre>
form :: Array (Text, Text) -> Body

-- | Create a raw body with custom content type.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Http.raw "application<i>xml" "<a>order</a><a>id</a>123&lt;</i>id&gt;<a>/order</a>"
--   </pre>
raw :: Text -> Text -> Body

-- | No request body.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Http.Request { body = Http.noBody, ... }
--   </pre>
noBody :: Body

-- | Default retry configuration.
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>3 attempts (1 initial + 2 retries)</li>
--   <li>1 second initial delay</li>
--   <li>30 second max delay</li>
--   <li>Retries on: 429, 500, 502, 503, 504</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   <pre>
--   Http.Request
--     { ...
--     , retry = Http.defaultRetry
--     }
--   </pre>
defaultRetry :: Retry

-- | No retries - fail immediately on any error.
--   
--   Use for fire-and-forget requests where retries don't make sense:
--   
--   <pre>
--   -- Slack webhook - fire and forget
--   Http.Request
--     { ...
--     , retry = Http.noRetry
--     }
--   </pre>
noRetry :: Retry

-- | Custom retry count with default backoff settings.
--   
--   <pre>
--   -- 5 attempts with default delays
--   Http.Request
--     { ...
--     , retry = Http.withRetries 5
--     }
--   </pre>
withRetries :: Int -> Retry


module Integration.Brevo.Internal
toHttpRequest :: Request command -> Request command
encodeRequest :: Request command -> Text
data BrevoResponseHandler command
BrevoResponseHandler :: (Response -> command) -> (Text -> command) -> BrevoResponseHandler command
[onSuccess] :: BrevoResponseHandler command -> Response -> command
[onError] :: BrevoResponseHandler command -> Text -> command


-- | Internal implementation for ACS transactional email integration.
--   
--   Contains the transformation from <a>Request</a> to <a>Request</a>, the
--   single <a>unwrap</a> site for the Bearer token, the endpoint guard
--   (SEC-001), and the <a>AcsResponseHandler</a> HTTP response dispatcher.
--   
--   <b>This module is not re-exported from <a>Acs</a>.</b>

module Integration.Acs.Internal

-- | Convert a validated Request into an Http.Request, unwrapping the
--   Bearer token. This is the only site where <a>unwrap</a> is called
--   (single-unwrap invariant). Called from: <a>toAction</a> (after
--   <a>validateEndpoint</a> passes).
toHttpRequest :: Request command -> Request command

-- | Pure planning step for the <a>ToAction</a> instance: validate the
--   endpoint (SEC-001) and, on success, build the <a>Request</a> from the
--   trimmed endpoint. Returns <a>Err</a> with the guard message for a
--   non-https endpoint (the token is never unwrapped), <a>Ok</a> the
--   request otherwise. Exposed for testing so both branches are assertable
--   without a live HTTP call.
planAction :: Request command -> Result Text (Request command)

-- | Encode a Request into ACS-compatible JSON wire format. Target shape:
--   
--   <pre>
--   { "senderAddress": "noreply@myapp.com"
--   , "content": { "subject": <a>Welcome</a>, "html": "&lt;h1&gt;Hi&lt;/h1&gt;" }
--   , "recipients": { "to": [ { "address": "user@example.com" } ] }
--   }
--   </pre>
--   
--   Hand-built to omit optional fields entirely (never serialize null).
--   Called from: <a>toHttpRequest</a>.
encodeRequest :: Request command -> Text

-- | Pick the ACS body key for the chosen Body variant. Returns an array
--   with a single (key, value) pair: <tt>("html", ...)</tt> or
--   <tt>("plainText", ...)</tt>.
encodeBodyField :: Body -> Array (Text, Value)

-- | Encode a Recipient as a JSON object <tt>{ "address": "..." }</tt>,
--   adding <tt>"displayName"</tt> when the address carries a name (ACS
--   <tt>EmailAddress</tt> shape). The optional name is only omitted when
--   absent — never silently dropped.
encodeRecipientAddress :: Recipient -> Value

-- | Extract the email address from a Sender.
senderEmail :: Sender -> Text

-- | Accept only an https endpoint; anything else would leak the Bearer
--   token. Returns <a>Ok</a> with the trimmed endpoint on success,
--   <a>Err</a> with a guided message on non-https. Called from:
--   <a>toAction</a> (before token unwrap). ADR §4 (SEC-001): ensures the
--   Bearer token is never transmitted over cleartext.
validateEndpoint :: Text -> Result Text Text

-- | Best-effort ACS operation id: read from body <tt>id</tt> key, fall
--   back to <tt>Operation-Location</tt> header trailing segment, fall back
--   to empty string. Called from <a>handleAcsResponse</a> on every 202.
operationIdFrom :: Response -> Text

-- | Parse the operation id from an Operation-Location header value. The
--   header value is typically something like
--   <tt><a>https://my-acs.communication.azure.com/operations/abc-123</a></tt>;
--   extract the last path segment.
operationIdFromLocation :: Text -> Text

-- | Dispatch the ACS HTTP response on status code. 202 Accepted always
--   succeeds (never retried, ADR §6, SEC-002). Auth/error cases return
--   sanitized messages (no token or body leak). Called from:
--   <a>toHttpRequest</a> as the onSuccess handler for <a>Request</a>.
handleAcsResponse :: AcsResponseHandler command -> Response -> command

-- | Internal record bundling the onSuccess and onError callbacks from the
--   Request. Used to pass callbacks through to <a>handleAcsResponse</a>
--   without exposing them outside Internal.
data AcsResponseHandler command
AcsResponseHandler :: (Response -> command) -> (Text -> command) -> AcsResponseHandler command
[onSuccess] :: AcsResponseHandler command -> Response -> command
[onError] :: AcsResponseHandler command -> Text -> command
instance (Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON command, GHC.TypeLits.KnownSymbol (Service.Command.Core.NameOf command)) => Integration.ToAction (Integration.Acs.Request.Request command)


module Integration.Acs

-- | A wire-format email address: optional display name + email string.
data Address
Address :: Maybe Text -> Text -> Address
[name] :: Address -> Maybe Text
[email] :: Address -> Text

-- | The sender address. A newtype over <a>Address</a> to prevent
--   accidental swap with <a>Recipient</a>. <a>Show</a> redacts the email
--   (no PII leak).
newtype Sender
Sender :: Address -> Sender

-- | A recipient address. A newtype over <a>Address</a> to prevent
--   accidental swap with <a>Sender</a>. <a>Show</a> redacts the email (no
--   PII leak).
newtype Recipient
Recipient :: Address -> Recipient

-- | Build a sender from just an email address (no display name).
--   
--   <pre>
--   Acs.sender "noreply@myapp.com"
--   </pre>
sender :: Text -> Sender

-- | Build a recipient from just an email address (no display name).
--   
--   <pre>
--   Acs.recipient "user@example.com"
--   </pre>
recipient :: Text -> Recipient

-- | Mutually-exclusive email body. ACS rejects a request that sets both
--   <tt>html</tt> and <tt>plainText</tt>; the sum enforces the invariant
--   at compile time.
data Body
HtmlBody :: Text -> Body
TextBody :: Text -> Body

-- | Send one transactional email through an ACS Email resource. Reads the
--   Entra Bearer token from <tt>?config.acsAccessToken</tt>. The endpoint
--   must use <tt>https://</tt> — a non-https endpoint is reported through
--   the error callback without ever unwrapping the token.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Acs.send "<a>https://my-acs.communication.azure.com"</a>
--     (Acs.sender "noreply@myapp.com") (Acs.recipient info.email)
--     "Your order is confirmed" (Acs.HtmlBody "&lt;h1&gt;Thanks&lt;/h1&gt;")
--     (\r -&gt; EmailSent { operationId = r.operationId }) (\e -&gt; EmailFailed { reason = e })
--     |&gt; Integration.outbound
--   </pre>
send :: forall command config. (?config :: config, HasField "acsAccessToken" config (Redacted Text)) => Text -> Sender -> Recipient -> Text -> Body -> (Response -> command) -> (Text -> command) -> Request command

-- | A complete ACS email send, produced by <a>send</a> and consumed by
--   <a>outbound</a>. <a>accessToken</a> is 'Redacted Text' so the Entra
--   Bearer token is never logged or serialized by accident.
data Request command
Request :: Text -> Sender -> Array Recipient -> Text -> Body -> Redacted Text -> (Response -> command) -> (Text -> command) -> Request command
[endpoint] :: Request command -> Text
[sender] :: Request command -> Sender
[to] :: Request command -> Array Recipient
[subject] :: Request command -> Text
[body] :: Request command -> Body
[accessToken] :: Request command -> Redacted Text
[onSuccess] :: Request command -> Response -> command
[onError] :: Request command -> Text -> command

-- | ACS's success payload for an accepted email send. ACS is asynchronous;
--   <a>operationId</a> identifies the send for later delivery-status
--   polling. Decoded from the ACS wire field <tt>id</tt> (not
--   <tt>operationId</tt>).
data Response
Response :: Text -> Response
[operationId] :: Response -> Text


-- | # AI-Powered Document/Image Transcription Integration
--   
--   This module provides AI-powered document/image text extraction using
--   multimodal models (Gemini, Claude Vision, GPT-4o) via the OpenRouter
--   API.
--   
--   <h2>Two-Persona Model</h2>
--   
--   Following ADR-0008 and ADR-0023, this integration separates concerns:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><b>Jess (Integration User)</b>: Configures extraction with pure
--   records. No <tt>Task</tt>, no API calls, no base64 encoding
--   visible.</li>
--   <li><b>Nick (Integration Developer)</b>: Implements <tt>ToAction</tt>
--   with file retrieval, base64 encoding, and OpenRouter orchestration in
--   <a>Integration.Ocr.Ai.Internal</a>.</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   <h2>Quick Start</h2>
--   
--   <pre>
--   import Integration qualified
--   import Integration.Ocr.Ai qualified as OcrAi
--   
--   documentIntegrations :: Document -&gt; DocumentEvent -&gt; Integration.Outbound
--   documentIntegrations doc event = case event of
--     PdfUploaded info -&gt; Integration.batch
--       [ Integration.outbound OcrAi.Request
--           { fileRef = info.fileRef
--           , mimeType = "application/pdf"
--           , model = "google/gemini-pro-1.5"
--           , config = OcrAi.defaultConfig
--           , onSuccess = \result -&gt; RecordTranscription
--               { documentId = doc.id
--               , text = result.text
--               }
--           , onError = \err -&gt; TranscriptionFailed
--               { documentId = doc.id
--               , error = err
--               }
--           }
--       ]
--     _ -&gt; Integration.none
--   </pre>
--   
--   <h2>Extraction Modes</h2>
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><a>FullText</a>: Extract all text faithfully, preserving
--   structure</li>
--   <li><a>Summary</a>: Summarize document content</li>
--   <li><a>Structured</a>: Extract as structured JSON</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   <h2>Companion Module</h2>
--   
--   For CLI-based extraction (faster, free, but limited to digital PDFs),
--   see <a>Integration.Pdf.ExtractText</a>.
--   
--   <h2>Data Privacy Notice</h2>
--   
--   File content is sent to third-party AI providers via OpenRouter.
--   Ensure appropriate data processing agreements are in place. See
--   ADR-0023 for security and privacy considerations.
--   
--   <h2>Known Limitations (v1)</h2>
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>No streaming support</li>
--   <li>No confidence scoring (always Nothing)</li>
--   <li>No page-level extraction (whole document only)</li>
--   </ul>

module Integration.Ocr.Ai

-- | The main request configuration record that Jess instantiates.
--   
--   The <tt>command</tt> type parameter is the domain command emitted by
--   <a>onSuccess</a> or <a>onError</a> callbacks.
--   
--   <h2>Fields</h2>
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><a>fileRef</a>: Reference to the uploaded document/image file</li>
--   <li><a>mimeType</a>: MIME type of the file (e.g.,
--   <tt>"application/pdf"</tt>, <tt>"image/png"</tt>)</li>
--   <li><a>model</a>: AI model to use (e.g.,
--   <tt>"google/gemini-pro-1.5"</tt>)</li>
--   <li><a>config</a>: Extraction configuration (use
--   <a>defaultConfig</a>)</li>
--   <li><a>onSuccess</a>: Callback that receives result and returns a
--   domain command</li>
--   <li><a>onError</a>: Callback for error handling</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   <h2>Example</h2>
--   
--   <pre>
--   Integration.outbound OcrAi.Request
--     { fileRef = e.uploadedFile
--     , mimeType = "application/pdf"
--     , model = "google/gemini-pro-1.5"
--     , config = OcrAi.defaultConfig
--         { extractionMode = OcrAi.Summary
--         }
--     , onSuccess = \result -&gt; RecordText
--         { documentId = doc.id
--         , text = result.text
--         }
--     , onError = \err -&gt; TranscriptionFailed
--         { documentId = doc.id
--         , error = err
--         }
--     }
--   </pre>
data Request command
Request :: FileRef -> Text -> Text -> Config -> (TranscriptionResult -> command) -> (Text -> command) -> Request command

-- | Reference to the uploaded document/image file
[fileRef] :: Request command -> FileRef

-- | MIME type of the file (e.g., "application/pdf", "image/png")
[mimeType] :: Request command -> Text

-- | AI model to use (e.g., "google/gemini-pro-1.5")
[model] :: Request command -> Text

-- | Extraction configuration
[config] :: Request command -> Config

-- | Callback for successful transcription
[onSuccess] :: Request command -> TranscriptionResult -> command

-- | Callback for transcription errors
[onError] :: Request command -> Text -> command

-- | Configuration for AI transcription.
--   
--   Use <a>defaultConfig</a> for sensible defaults, then override specific
--   fields:
--   
--   <pre>
--   OcrAi.defaultConfig
--     { extractionMode = OcrAi.Summary
--     , language = Just "es"
--     }
--   </pre>
data Config
Config :: ExtractionMode -> Maybe Text -> Maybe Int -> Maybe Text -> Int -> Config

-- | How to extract text (default: FullText)
[extractionMode] :: Config -> ExtractionMode

-- | Language hint for the AI model (e.g., "en", "es")
[language] :: Config -> Maybe Text

-- | Limit number of pages to process (Nothing = all)
[maxPages] :: Config -> Maybe Int

-- | Override the default system prompt
[systemPrompt] :: Config -> Maybe Text

-- | Timeout for the API call (default: 120)
[timeoutSeconds] :: Config -> Int

-- | Extraction modes for AI-powered transcription.
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><a>FullText</a>: Extract all text faithfully, preserving
--   structure. Best for documents where you need the complete text.</li>
--   <li><a>Summary</a>: Summarize document content. Best for getting a
--   quick overview of long documents.</li>
--   <li><a>Structured</a>: Extract as structured JSON. Best for forms,
--   invoices, and documents with known structure.</li>
--   </ul>
data ExtractionMode

-- | Extract all text faithfully, preserving structure
FullText :: ExtractionMode

-- | Summarize document content
Summary :: ExtractionMode

-- | Extract as structured JSON
Structured :: ExtractionMode

-- | Result of AI-powered transcription.
--   
--   <pre>
--   onSuccess = \result -&gt; RecordTranscription
--     { text = result.text
--     , pageCount = result.pageCount
--     }
--   </pre>
data TranscriptionResult
TranscriptionResult :: Text -> Maybe Int -> Maybe Float -> TranscriptionResult

-- | Extracted/transcribed text
[text] :: TranscriptionResult -> Text

-- | Number of pages processed (if available from model response)
[pageCount] :: TranscriptionResult -> Maybe Int

-- | Confidence score (always Nothing for v1, Float is Double-precision in
--   NeoHaskell)
[confidence] :: TranscriptionResult -> Maybe Float

-- | Default configuration for AI transcription.
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>Extraction mode: FullText</li>
--   <li>Language: None (auto-detect)</li>
--   <li>Max pages: None (all pages)</li>
--   <li>System prompt: None (use default)</li>
--   <li>Timeout: 120 seconds</li>
--   </ul>
defaultConfig :: Config
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Integration.Ocr.Ai.Config
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Integration.Ocr.Ai.ExtractionMode
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Integration.Ocr.Ai.TranscriptionResult
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Integration.Ocr.Ai.Config
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Integration.Ocr.Ai.ExtractionMode
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Integration.Ocr.Ai.TranscriptionResult
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Integration.Ocr.Ai.Config
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Integration.Ocr.Ai.ExtractionMode
instance GHC.Generics.Generic (Integration.Ocr.Ai.Request command)
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Integration.Ocr.Ai.TranscriptionResult
instance GHC.Show.Show Integration.Ocr.Ai.Config
instance GHC.Show.Show Integration.Ocr.Ai.ExtractionMode
instance GHC.Show.Show Integration.Ocr.Ai.TranscriptionResult
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Integration.Ocr.Ai.Config
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Integration.Ocr.Ai.ExtractionMode
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Integration.Ocr.Ai.TranscriptionResult


-- | Message types for OpenRouter chat completions.
--   
--   This module provides the <a>Message</a> and <a>Role</a> types for
--   building conversations with AI models via OpenRouter.
--   
--   <h2>Quick Start (Text-only)</h2>
--   
--   <pre>
--   import Integration.OpenRouter.Message (Message)
--   import Integration.OpenRouter.Message qualified as Message
--   
--   messages =
--     [ Message.system "You are a helpful assistant."
--     , Message.user "What is Haskell?"
--     , Message.assistant "Haskell is a functional programming language."
--     , Message.user "Tell me more."
--     ]
--   </pre>
--   
--   <h2>Multimodal (Images/PDFs)</h2>
--   
--   <pre>
--   import Integration.OpenRouter.Message qualified as Message
--   
--   -- Send a PDF attachment with a text prompt:
--   multimodalMsg = Message.userWithAttachment
--     "Extract all text from this document."
--     base64Content
--     "application/pdf"
--   </pre>
--   
--   <h2>Backward Compatibility</h2>
--   
--   Text-only messages serialize identically to the previous format:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><tt>Message.user "hello"</tt> →
--   <tt>{"role":"user","content":"hello"}</tt></li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   Multimodal messages use the OpenAI content array format:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><tt>Message.userWithAttachment ...</tt> →
--   <tt>{"role":"user","content":[...]}</tt></li>
--   </ul>

module Integration.OpenRouter.Message

-- | A single message in a conversation.
--   
--   Messages form the conversation history sent to the model. Use the
--   smart constructors <a>user</a>, <a>assistant</a>, and <a>system</a>
--   for convenient message creation.
--   
--   The <a>content</a> field supports both plain text and multimodal
--   content. Text-only messages serialize identically to the previous
--   format.
data Message
Message :: Role -> Content -> Message
[role] :: Message -> Role
[content] :: Message -> Content

-- | Role of a message participant in the conversation.
--   
--   OpenRouter (like OpenAI) uses roles to distinguish between:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><a>User</a> - The human asking questions</li>
--   <li><a>Assistant</a> - The AI responding</li>
--   <li><a>System</a> - Instructions that guide the AI's behavior</li>
--   </ul>
data Role
User :: Role
Assistant :: Role
System :: Role

-- | Message content — either plain text or multimodal content array.
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><a>TextContent</a> — Plain text (backward compatible with previous
--   API)</li>
--   <li><a>MultiContent</a> — Array of content parts for multimodal
--   messages</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   <h2>JSON Serialization</h2>
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><tt>TextContent "hello"</tt> → <tt>"hello"</tt> (plain JSON
--   string)</li>
--   <li><tt>MultiContent [...]</tt> → <tt>[{"type":"text",...}, ...]</tt>
--   (JSON array)</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   <tt>FromJSON</tt> accepts both formats for backward compatibility.
data Content

-- | Plain text content (backward compatible)
TextContent :: Text -> Content

-- | Array of content parts (text + images/attachments)
MultiContent :: Array ContentPart -> Content

-- | A single part within multimodal content.
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><a>TextPart</a> — A text segment</li>
--   <li><a>ImageUrlPart</a> — An image or document attachment</li>
--   </ul>
data ContentPart

-- | Text content part
TextPart :: Text -> ContentPart

-- | Image/attachment content part
ImageUrlPart :: ImageUrl -> ContentPart

-- | Image URL reference for multimodal content.
--   
--   Used to embed images or documents as data URLs:
--   
--   <pre>
--   ImageUrl { url = "data:application/pdf;base64,..." }
--   </pre>
data ImageUrl
ImageUrl :: Text -> ImageUrl

-- | Data URL (e.g., <tt>"data:application/pdf;base64,..."</tt>)
[url] :: ImageUrl -> Text

-- | Create a user message.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Message.user "What is the meaning of life?"
--   </pre>
user :: Text -> Message

-- | Create an assistant message.
--   
--   Useful for providing example responses or continuing a conversation:
--   
--   <pre>
--   Message.assistant "I'd be happy to help with that."
--   </pre>
assistant :: Text -> Message

-- | Create a system message.
--   
--   System messages set the AI's behavior and personality:
--   
--   <pre>
--   Message.system "You are a helpful coding assistant. Be concise."
--   </pre>
system :: Text -> Message

-- | Create a multimodal user message with an attachment.
--   
--   Builds a message with both a text prompt and an image/document
--   attachment encoded as a data URL.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Message.userWithAttachment
--     "Extract all text from this document."
--     base64Content
--     "application/pdf"
--   </pre>
userWithAttachment :: Text -> Text -> Text -> Message
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Integration.OpenRouter.Message.Content
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Integration.OpenRouter.Message.ContentPart
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Integration.OpenRouter.Message.ImageUrl
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Integration.OpenRouter.Message.Message
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Integration.OpenRouter.Message.Role
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Integration.OpenRouter.Message.Content
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Integration.OpenRouter.Message.ContentPart
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Integration.OpenRouter.Message.ImageUrl
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Integration.OpenRouter.Message.Message
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Integration.OpenRouter.Message.Role
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Integration.OpenRouter.Message.Content
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Integration.OpenRouter.Message.ContentPart
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Integration.OpenRouter.Message.ImageUrl
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Integration.OpenRouter.Message.Message
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Integration.OpenRouter.Message.Role
instance GHC.Show.Show Integration.OpenRouter.Message.Content
instance GHC.Show.Show Integration.OpenRouter.Message.ContentPart
instance GHC.Show.Show Integration.OpenRouter.Message.ImageUrl
instance GHC.Show.Show Integration.OpenRouter.Message.Message
instance GHC.Show.Show Integration.OpenRouter.Message.Role
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Integration.OpenRouter.Message.Content
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Integration.OpenRouter.Message.ContentPart
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Integration.OpenRouter.Message.ImageUrl
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Integration.OpenRouter.Message.Message
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Integration.OpenRouter.Message.Role


-- | Response types for OpenRouter chat completions.
--   
--   This module provides types for parsing responses from the OpenRouter
--   API. These types are what Jess receives in the <tt>onSuccess</tt>
--   callback.
--   
--   <h2>Response Structure</h2>
--   
--   A typical response contains:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><a>id</a> - Unique generation identifier</li>
--   <li><a>model</a> - The actual model that processed the request</li>
--   <li><a>choices</a> - Array of completion choices (usually one)</li>
--   <li><a>usage</a> - Token usage statistics (optional)</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   <h2>Example</h2>
--   
--   <pre>
--   onSuccess = \response -&gt; do
--     let firstChoice = response.choices |&gt; Array.first
--     case firstChoice of
--       Just choice -&gt; HandleResponse { content = choice.message.content }
--       Nothing -&gt; HandleError { error = "No choices in response" }
--   </pre>

module Integration.OpenRouter.Response

-- | Full response from OpenRouter chat completion API.
--   
--   This is what you receive in your <tt>onSuccess</tt> callback:
--   
--   <pre>
--   onSuccess = \response -&gt;
--     case response.choices |&gt; Array.first of
--       Just choice -&gt; GotAnswer { answer = choice.message.content }
--       Nothing -&gt; NoAnswer
--   </pre>
data Response
Response :: Text -> Text -> Array Choice -> Maybe Usage -> Response

-- | Unique generation identifier (e.g., "gen-abc123")
[id] :: Response -> Text

-- | The model that processed the request
[model] :: Response -> Text

-- | Completion choices (usually one element)
[choices] :: Response -> Array Choice

-- | Token usage statistics (may be absent)
[usage] :: Response -> Maybe Usage

-- | A single completion choice from the model.
--   
--   Most requests return a single choice. <a>toolCalls</a> is populated
--   for tool_choice = "required" requests, empty for regular chat
--   completion requests (backward compatible).
data Choice
Choice :: Message -> FinishReason -> {-# UNPACK #-}Int -> Array ToolCall -> Choice

-- | The assistant's response message
[message] :: Choice -> Message

-- | Why generation stopped
[finishReason] :: Choice -> FinishReason

-- | Index of this choice (0 for single responses)
[index] :: Choice -> {-# UNPACK #-}Int

-- | Tool calls made by the model (empty array for non-agent responses,
--   populated for tool_choice = "required" requests)
[toolCalls] :: Choice -> Array ToolCall

-- | Token usage statistics for the request.
--   
--   Useful for monitoring costs and optimizing prompts.
--   
--   <pre>
--   case response.usage of
--     Just usage -&gt; logUsage usage.totalTokens
--     Nothing -&gt; pure ()
--   </pre>
data Usage
Usage :: Int -> Int -> Int -> Usage

-- | Tokens used by the input messages
[promptTokens] :: Usage -> Int

-- | Tokens generated in the response
[completionTokens] :: Usage -> Int

-- | Total tokens (prompt + completion)
[totalTokens] :: Usage -> Int

-- | Why the model stopped generating tokens.
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><a>Stop</a> - Natural completion (hit stop sequence or end of
--   response)</li>
--   <li><a>Length</a> - Hit max_tokens limit</li>
--   <li><a>ContentFilter</a> - Content was filtered for safety</li>
--   <li><a>Unknown</a> - Provider returned an unrecognized reason</li>
--   </ul>
data FinishReason
Stop :: FinishReason
Length :: FinishReason
ContentFilter :: FinishReason
Unknown :: Text -> FinishReason

-- | A tool call returned by the model.
data ToolCall
ToolCall :: Text -> ToolCallFunction -> ToolCall

-- | Unique call identifier (e.g., "call_abc123")
[id] :: ToolCall -> Text

-- | The function name and encoded arguments
[function] :: ToolCall -> ToolCallFunction

-- | A function call made by the model.
--   
--   NOTE: <a>arguments</a> is wrapped in <a>Redacted</a> because it
--   contains AI-decoded command JSON with user-correlated data (cart IDs,
--   quantities, etc.) that must not appear in logs. Unwrap only inside
--   <tt>decodeArguments</tt>.
data ToolCallFunction
ToolCallFunction :: Text -> Redacted Text -> ToolCallFunction

-- | Name of the tool (matches <a>toolName</a>)
[name] :: ToolCallFunction -> Text

-- | JSON-encoded arguments string, redacted to prevent log leakage. Unwrap
--   with <a>unwrap</a> only inside <tt>decodeArguments</tt>.
[arguments] :: ToolCallFunction -> Redacted Text
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Integration.OpenRouter.Response.Choice
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Integration.OpenRouter.Response.FinishReason
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Integration.OpenRouter.Response.Response
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Integration.OpenRouter.Response.ToolCall
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Integration.OpenRouter.Response.ToolCallFunction
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Integration.OpenRouter.Response.Usage
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Integration.OpenRouter.Response.Choice
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Integration.OpenRouter.Response.FinishReason
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Integration.OpenRouter.Response.Response
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Integration.OpenRouter.Response.ToolCall
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Integration.OpenRouter.Response.ToolCallFunction
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Integration.OpenRouter.Response.Usage
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Integration.OpenRouter.Response.Choice
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Integration.OpenRouter.Response.FinishReason
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Integration.OpenRouter.Response.Response
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Integration.OpenRouter.Response.ToolCall
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Integration.OpenRouter.Response.ToolCallFunction
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Integration.OpenRouter.Response.Usage
instance GHC.Show.Show Integration.OpenRouter.Response.FinishReason
instance GHC.Show.Show Integration.OpenRouter.Response.Usage
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Integration.OpenRouter.Response.Choice
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Integration.OpenRouter.Response.FinishReason
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Integration.OpenRouter.Response.Response
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Integration.OpenRouter.Response.ToolCall
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Integration.OpenRouter.Response.ToolCallFunction
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Integration.OpenRouter.Response.Usage
