-- | Request types for OpenRouter chat completions.
--   
--   This module provides the <a>Request</a> type that Jess configures to
--   make chat completion requests via OpenRouter.
--   
--   <h2>Quick Start</h2>
--   
--   <pre>
--   import Integration qualified
--   import Integration.OpenRouter qualified as OpenRouter
--   import Integration.OpenRouter.Message qualified as Message
--   
--   -- Simple usage with smart constructor:
--   OpenRouter.chatCompletion
--     [Message.user "Hello!"]
--     "anthropic/claude-3.5-sonnet"
--     (\response -&gt; GotResponse {response})
--     (\err -&gt; GotError {err})
--     |&gt; Integration.outbound
--   </pre>
--   
--   <h2>Configuration</h2>
--   
--   For advanced options like temperature and max tokens, use the
--   <a>Request</a> record directly:
--   
--   <pre>
--   Integration.outbound OpenRouter.Request
--     { messages = myMessages
--     , model = "openai/gpt-4o"
--     , config = OpenRouter.defaultConfig
--         { temperature = Just 0.9
--         , maxTokens = Just 500
--         }
--     , onSuccess = handleSuccess
--     , onError = handleError
--     }
--   </pre>

module Integration.OpenRouter.Request

-- | Chat completion request configuration.
--   
--   This is what Jess (the user) builds. The integration transforms this
--   into an <a>Request</a> via <tt>toHttpRequest</tt>.
--   
--   <h2>Fields</h2>
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><a>messages</a> - The conversation history (required)</li>
--   <li><a>model</a> - Model identifier like
--   <tt>"anthropic/claude-3.5-sonnet"</tt> (required)</li>
--   <li><a>config</a> - Optional parameters (temperature, max_tokens,
--   etc.)</li>
--   <li><a>onSuccess</a> - Command to emit when API returns successfully
--   (required)</li>
--   <li><a>onError</a> - Command to emit on failure (required)</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   <h2>Example</h2>
--   
--   <pre>
--   Integration.outbound OpenRouter.Request
--     { messages = [Message.system "Be concise.", Message.user question]
--     , model = "anthropic/claude-3.5-sonnet"
--     , config = OpenRouter.defaultConfig
--     , onSuccess = \response -&gt; GotAnswer { response }
--     , onError = \err -&gt; AiError { err }
--     }
--   </pre>
data Request command
Request :: Array Message -> Text -> Config -> (Response -> command) -> (Text -> command) -> Request command

-- | The conversation history
[messages] :: Request command -> Array Message

-- | Model identifier (e.g., "anthropic<i>claude-3.5-sonnet",
--   "openai</i>gpt-4o")
[model] :: Request command -> Text

-- | Optional parameters (temperature, max_tokens, etc.)
[config] :: Request command -> Config

-- | Command to emit when the API returns successfully
[onSuccess] :: Request command -> Response -> command

-- | Command to emit on failure (network error, parse error, API error)
[onError] :: Request command -> Text -> command

-- | Optional configuration for chat completion requests.
--   
--   Use <a>defaultConfig</a> as a starting point and override fields as
--   needed:
--   
--   <pre>
--   config = OpenRouter.defaultConfig
--     { temperature = Just 0.7
--     , maxTokens = Just 1000
--     }
--   </pre>
data Config
Config :: Maybe Float -> Maybe Int -> Maybe Float -> Maybe Float -> Maybe Float -> Maybe Text -> Maybe Text -> Int -> Config

-- | Sampling temperature (0.0-2.0). Higher = more creative. Default:
--   model-dependent (usually 1.0)
[temperature] :: Config -> Maybe Float

-- | Maximum tokens to generate. Default: model-dependent
[maxTokens] :: Config -> Maybe Int

-- | Nucleus sampling parameter (0.0-1.0). Default: 1.0
[topP] :: Config -> Maybe Float

-- | Penalize repeated tokens based on frequency (-2.0 to 2.0)
[frequencyPenalty] :: Config -> Maybe Float

-- | Penalize tokens based on presence in text so far (-2.0 to 2.0)
[presencePenalty] :: Config -> Maybe Float

-- | HTTP-Referer header for OpenRouter rankings (your site URL)
[referer] :: Config -> Maybe Text

-- | X-Title header for OpenRouter rankings (your app name)
[title] :: Config -> Maybe Text

-- | Request timeout in seconds. Default: 60
[timeoutSeconds] :: Config -> Int

-- | Sensible default configuration.
--   
--   All optional parameters are <a>Nothing</a> (use model defaults).
--   Timeout is set to 60 seconds.
--   
--   <pre>
--   config = OpenRouter.defaultConfig { temperature = Just 0.5 }
--   </pre>
defaultConfig :: Config

-- | Smart constructor for creating a basic chat completion request.
--   
--   Uses <a>defaultConfig</a> for optional parameters. For custom
--   configuration, use the <a>Request</a> record constructor directly.
--   
--   <pre>
--   request = OpenRouter.chatCompletion
--     [Message.user "Hello!"]
--     "anthropic/claude-3.5-sonnet"
--     (\response -&gt; GotResponse {response})
--     (\err -&gt; GotError {err})
--   </pre>
chatCompletion :: Array Message -> Text -> (Response -> command) -> (Text -> command) -> Request command
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Integration.OpenRouter.Request.Config
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Integration.OpenRouter.Request.Config
instance GHC.Show.Show Integration.OpenRouter.Request.Config


-- | Internal implementation for OpenRouter integration.
--   
--   This module contains Nick's code - the transformation from
--   <a>Request</a> to <a>Request</a>.
--   
--   <b>This module exports <a>toHttpRequest</a> for the public API.</b>

module Integration.OpenRouter.Internal

-- | Transform an OpenRouter.Request into an Http.Request.
--   
--   This is the core of the piggyback pattern. We build an <a>Request</a>
--   that the existing HTTP integration will execute.
--   
--   <h2>Usage</h2>
--   
--   <pre>
--   let openRouterRequest = OpenRouter.chatCompletion { ... }
--   let httpRequest = OpenRouter.toHttpRequest openRouterRequest
--   Integration.outbound httpRequest
--   </pre>
toHttpRequest :: Request command -> Request command

-- | Internal JSON body sent to OpenRouter API.
--   
--   This is Nick's concern - Jess never sees this type.
data RequestBody
RequestBody :: Array Message -> Text -> Bool -> Maybe Float -> Maybe Int -> Maybe Float -> Maybe Float -> Maybe Float -> Maybe (Array Value) -> Maybe Text -> RequestBody
[messages] :: RequestBody -> Array Message
[model] :: RequestBody -> Text
[stream] :: RequestBody -> Bool
[temperature] :: RequestBody -> Maybe Float
[max_tokens] :: RequestBody -> Maybe Int
[top_p] :: RequestBody -> Maybe Float
[frequency_penalty] :: RequestBody -> Maybe Float
[presence_penalty] :: RequestBody -> Maybe Float

-- | Tool definitions array (Nothing = omit field, preserving current
--   behaviour)
[tools] :: RequestBody -> Maybe (Array Value)

-- | "required" forces the model to call a tool (Nothing = omit field)
[tool_choice] :: RequestBody -> Maybe Text

-- | Build optional headers (Referer, X-Title).
buildHeaders :: Config -> Array (Text, Text)

-- | Parse the HTTP response and invoke the user's onSuccess callback.
handleSuccess :: Request command -> Response -> command

-- | Handle HTTP errors by invoking the user's onError callback.
handleError :: Request command -> Text -> command
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Integration.OpenRouter.Internal.RequestBody
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Integration.OpenRouter.Internal.RequestBody
instance GHC.Show.Show Integration.OpenRouter.Internal.RequestBody
instance (Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON command, GHC.TypeLits.KnownSymbol (Service.Command.Core.NameOf command)) => Integration.ToAction (Integration.OpenRouter.Request.Request command)
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Integration.OpenRouter.Internal.RequestBody


module Integration.OpenRouter

-- | Chat completion request configuration.
--   
--   This is what Jess (the user) builds. The integration transforms this
--   into an <a>Request</a> via <tt>toHttpRequest</tt>.
--   
--   <h2>Fields</h2>
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><a>messages</a> - The conversation history (required)</li>
--   <li><a>model</a> - Model identifier like
--   <tt>"anthropic/claude-3.5-sonnet"</tt> (required)</li>
--   <li><a>config</a> - Optional parameters (temperature, max_tokens,
--   etc.)</li>
--   <li><a>onSuccess</a> - Command to emit when API returns successfully
--   (required)</li>
--   <li><a>onError</a> - Command to emit on failure (required)</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   <h2>Example</h2>
--   
--   <pre>
--   Integration.outbound OpenRouter.Request
--     { messages = [Message.system "Be concise.", Message.user question]
--     , model = "anthropic/claude-3.5-sonnet"
--     , config = OpenRouter.defaultConfig
--     , onSuccess = \response -&gt; GotAnswer { response }
--     , onError = \err -&gt; AiError { err }
--     }
--   </pre>
data Request command
Request :: Array Message -> Text -> Config -> (Response -> command) -> (Text -> command) -> Request command

-- | The conversation history
[messages] :: Request command -> Array Message

-- | Model identifier (e.g., "anthropic<i>claude-3.5-sonnet",
--   "openai</i>gpt-4o")
[model] :: Request command -> Text

-- | Optional parameters (temperature, max_tokens, etc.)
[config] :: Request command -> Config

-- | Command to emit when the API returns successfully
[onSuccess] :: Request command -> Response -> command

-- | Command to emit on failure (network error, parse error, API error)
[onError] :: Request command -> Text -> command

-- | Optional configuration for chat completion requests.
--   
--   Use <a>defaultConfig</a> as a starting point and override fields as
--   needed:
--   
--   <pre>
--   config = OpenRouter.defaultConfig
--     { temperature = Just 0.7
--     , maxTokens = Just 1000
--     }
--   </pre>
data Config
Config :: Maybe Float -> Maybe Int -> Maybe Float -> Maybe Float -> Maybe Float -> Maybe Text -> Maybe Text -> Int -> Config

-- | Sampling temperature (0.0-2.0). Higher = more creative. Default:
--   model-dependent (usually 1.0)
[temperature] :: Config -> Maybe Float

-- | Maximum tokens to generate. Default: model-dependent
[maxTokens] :: Config -> Maybe Int

-- | Nucleus sampling parameter (0.0-1.0). Default: 1.0
[topP] :: Config -> Maybe Float

-- | Penalize repeated tokens based on frequency (-2.0 to 2.0)
[frequencyPenalty] :: Config -> Maybe Float

-- | Penalize tokens based on presence in text so far (-2.0 to 2.0)
[presencePenalty] :: Config -> Maybe Float

-- | HTTP-Referer header for OpenRouter rankings (your site URL)
[referer] :: Config -> Maybe Text

-- | X-Title header for OpenRouter rankings (your app name)
[title] :: Config -> Maybe Text

-- | Request timeout in seconds. Default: 60
[timeoutSeconds] :: Config -> Int

-- | Sensible default configuration.
--   
--   All optional parameters are <a>Nothing</a> (use model defaults).
--   Timeout is set to 60 seconds.
--   
--   <pre>
--   config = OpenRouter.defaultConfig { temperature = Just 0.5 }
--   </pre>
defaultConfig :: Config

-- | Smart constructor for creating a basic chat completion request.
--   
--   Uses <a>defaultConfig</a> for optional parameters. For custom
--   configuration, use the <a>Request</a> record constructor directly.
--   
--   <pre>
--   request = OpenRouter.chatCompletion
--     [Message.user "Hello!"]
--     "anthropic/claude-3.5-sonnet"
--     (\response -&gt; GotResponse {response})
--     (\err -&gt; GotError {err})
--   </pre>
chatCompletion :: Array Message -> Text -> (Response -> command) -> (Text -> command) -> Request command

-- | Full response from OpenRouter chat completion API.
--   
--   This is what you receive in your <tt>onSuccess</tt> callback:
--   
--   <pre>
--   onSuccess = \response -&gt;
--     case response.choices |&gt; Array.first of
--       Just choice -&gt; GotAnswer { answer = choice.message.content }
--       Nothing -&gt; NoAnswer
--   </pre>
data Response
Response :: Text -> Text -> Array Choice -> Maybe Usage -> Response

-- | Unique generation identifier (e.g., "gen-abc123")
[id] :: Response -> Text

-- | The model that processed the request
[model] :: Response -> Text

-- | Completion choices (usually one element)
[choices] :: Response -> Array Choice

-- | Token usage statistics (may be absent)
[usage] :: Response -> Maybe Usage

-- | A single completion choice from the model.
--   
--   Most requests return a single choice. <a>toolCalls</a> is populated
--   for tool_choice = "required" requests, empty for regular chat
--   completion requests (backward compatible).
data Choice
Choice :: Message -> FinishReason -> {-# UNPACK #-}Int -> Array ToolCall -> Choice

-- | The assistant's response message
[message] :: Choice -> Message

-- | Why generation stopped
[finishReason] :: Choice -> FinishReason

-- | Index of this choice (0 for single responses)
[index] :: Choice -> {-# UNPACK #-}Int

-- | Tool calls made by the model (empty array for non-agent responses,
--   populated for tool_choice = "required" requests)
[toolCalls] :: Choice -> Array ToolCall

-- | Token usage statistics for the request.
--   
--   Useful for monitoring costs and optimizing prompts.
--   
--   <pre>
--   case response.usage of
--     Just usage -&gt; logUsage usage.totalTokens
--     Nothing -&gt; pure ()
--   </pre>
data Usage
Usage :: Int -> Int -> Int -> Usage

-- | Tokens used by the input messages
[promptTokens] :: Usage -> Int

-- | Tokens generated in the response
[completionTokens] :: Usage -> Int

-- | Total tokens (prompt + completion)
[totalTokens] :: Usage -> Int

-- | Why the model stopped generating tokens.
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><a>Stop</a> - Natural completion (hit stop sequence or end of
--   response)</li>
--   <li><a>Length</a> - Hit max_tokens limit</li>
--   <li><a>ContentFilter</a> - Content was filtered for safety</li>
--   <li><a>Unknown</a> - Provider returned an unrecognized reason</li>
--   </ul>
data FinishReason
Stop :: FinishReason
Length :: FinishReason
ContentFilter :: FinishReason
Unknown :: Text -> FinishReason

-- | A single message in a conversation.
--   
--   Messages form the conversation history sent to the model. Use the
--   smart constructors <a>user</a>, <a>assistant</a>, and <a>system</a>
--   for convenient message creation.
--   
--   The <a>content</a> field supports both plain text and multimodal
--   content. Text-only messages serialize identically to the previous
--   format.
data Message
Message :: Role -> Content -> Message
[role] :: Message -> Role
[content] :: Message -> Content

-- | Role of a message participant in the conversation.
--   
--   OpenRouter (like OpenAI) uses roles to distinguish between:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><a>User</a> - The human asking questions</li>
--   <li><a>Assistant</a> - The AI responding</li>
--   <li><a>System</a> - Instructions that guide the AI's behavior</li>
--   </ul>
data Role
User :: Role
Assistant :: Role
System :: Role

-- | Message content — either plain text or multimodal content array.
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><a>TextContent</a> — Plain text (backward compatible with previous
--   API)</li>
--   <li><a>MultiContent</a> — Array of content parts for multimodal
--   messages</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   <h2>JSON Serialization</h2>
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><tt>TextContent "hello"</tt> → <tt>"hello"</tt> (plain JSON
--   string)</li>
--   <li><tt>MultiContent [...]</tt> → <tt>[{"type":"text",...}, ...]</tt>
--   (JSON array)</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   <tt>FromJSON</tt> accepts both formats for backward compatibility.
data Content

-- | Plain text content (backward compatible)
TextContent :: Text -> Content

-- | Array of content parts (text + images/attachments)
MultiContent :: Array ContentPart -> Content

-- | A single part within multimodal content.
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><a>TextPart</a> — A text segment</li>
--   <li><a>ImageUrlPart</a> — An image or document attachment</li>
--   </ul>
data ContentPart

-- | Text content part
TextPart :: Text -> ContentPart

-- | Image/attachment content part
ImageUrlPart :: ImageUrl -> ContentPart

-- | Image URL reference for multimodal content.
--   
--   Used to embed images or documents as data URLs:
--   
--   <pre>
--   ImageUrl { url = "data:application/pdf;base64,..." }
--   </pre>
data ImageUrl
ImageUrl :: Text -> ImageUrl

-- | Data URL (e.g., <tt>"data:application/pdf;base64,..."</tt>)
[url] :: ImageUrl -> Text

-- | Create a user message.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Message.user "What is the meaning of life?"
--   </pre>
user :: Text -> Message

-- | Create an assistant message.
--   
--   Useful for providing example responses or continuing a conversation:
--   
--   <pre>
--   Message.assistant "I'd be happy to help with that."
--   </pre>
assistant :: Text -> Message

-- | Create a system message.
--   
--   System messages set the AI's behavior and personality:
--   
--   <pre>
--   Message.system "You are a helpful coding assistant. Be concise."
--   </pre>
system :: Text -> Message

-- | Create a multimodal user message with an attachment.
--   
--   Builds a message with both a text prompt and an image/document
--   attachment encoded as a data URL.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Message.userWithAttachment
--     "Extract all text from this document."
--     base64Content
--     "application/pdf"
--   </pre>
userWithAttachment :: Text -> Text -> Text -> Message

-- | Transform an OpenRouter.Request into an Http.Request.
--   
--   This is the core of the piggyback pattern. We build an <a>Request</a>
--   that the existing HTTP integration will execute.
--   
--   <h2>Usage</h2>
--   
--   <pre>
--   let openRouterRequest = OpenRouter.chatCompletion { ... }
--   let httpRequest = OpenRouter.toHttpRequest openRouterRequest
--   Integration.outbound httpRequest
--   </pre>
toHttpRequest :: Request command -> Request command


module Integration.Ocr.Ai.Internal
buildSystemPrompt :: Config -> Text
buildExtractionPrompt :: Config -> Text
fileAccessToIntegrationError :: FileAccessError -> IntegrationError
integrationErrorToText :: IntegrationError -> Text
instance (Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON command, GHC.TypeLits.KnownSymbol (Service.Command.Core.NameOf command)) => Integration.ToAction (Integration.Ocr.Ai.Request command)


-- | Request types and endpoint validation for Azure AI chat completions.
--   
--   Provides <a>AzureEndpoint</a> (validated by construction),
--   <a>Config</a>, <a>Request</a>, and the <a>chatCompletion</a> smart
--   constructor for the integration's callers.

module Integration.AzureAI.Request

-- | A validated Azure resource endpoint (https:// + allowed host suffix).
--   The constructor is NOT exported: use <a>azureEndpoint</a> or
--   <a>azureEndpointAllowing</a>. SEC-001: closed by construction — no
--   external caller can forge a host.
data AzureEndpoint

-- | Build an endpoint validated against the DEFAULT Azure host suffixes.
--   Delegates to <a>azureEndpointAllowing</a> with no extra suffixes.
--   
--   <pre>
--   case AzureAI.azureEndpoint "<a>https://my-res.openai.azure.com"</a> of
--     Result.Ok endpoint -&gt; useEndpoint endpoint
--     Result.Err reason  -&gt; logRejected reason
--   </pre>
azureEndpoint :: Text -> Result Text AzureEndpoint

-- | Same validation, plus caller-supplied ADDITIONAL host suffixes for
--   APIM custom domains / Private Endpoints. The single Result-returning
--   construction path. SEC-001: https scheme enforced; host must end with
--   an allowlisted suffix. SEC-002: <a>hostOf</a> strips query/fragment
--   before the suffix match.
--   
--   <pre>
--   AzureAI.azureEndpointAllowing
--     ["ai.contoso-internal.net"]
--     "<a>https://gateway.ai.contoso-internal.net"</a>
--   </pre>
azureEndpointAllowing :: Array Text -> Text -> Result Text AzureEndpoint

-- | Default allowed host suffixes: public Azure AI hosts plus sovereign
--   clouds.
--   
--   <pre>
--   -- openai.azure.com, cognitiveservices.azure.com, services.ai.azure.com,
--   -- inference.ai.azure.com, azure.us (Government), azure.cn (21Vianet)
--   AzureAI.defaultAzureHostSuffixes
--   </pre>
defaultAzureHostSuffixes :: Array Text

-- | Primary smart constructor. Sources the API key from
--   ?config.azureAiApiKey; seeds Config.endpoint from the validated
--   AzureEndpoint argument; apiVersion + tuning come from defaultConfig
--   (override via the Request/Config record).
--   
--   <pre>
--   case AzureAI.azureEndpoint "<a>https://my-res.openai.azure.com"</a> of
--     Result.Ok endpoint -&gt;
--       AzureAI.chatCompletion
--         endpoint
--         [AzureAI.system "Be concise.", AzureAI.user question]
--         "gpt-4o"
--         (\response -&gt; GotAnswer { response })
--         (\err -&gt; AiError { err })
--         |&gt; Integration.outbound
--     Result.Err reason -&gt;
--       Integration.none
--   </pre>
chatCompletion :: forall command config. (?config :: config, HasField "azureAiApiKey" config (Redacted Text)) => AzureEndpoint -> Array Message -> Text -> (Response -> command) -> (Text -> command) -> Request command

-- | The request the caller builds; consumed by Integration.outbound.
--   <a>apiKey</a> is 'Redacted Text', sourced from ?config by
--   <a>chatCompletion</a>. No Show/ToJSON — the unwrapped key can never
--   leak via a derived instance.
data Request command
Request :: Array Message -> Text -> Config -> Redacted Text -> (Response -> command) -> (Text -> command) -> Request command
[messages] :: Request command -> Array Message
[model] :: Request command -> Text
[config] :: Request command -> Config
[apiKey] :: Request command -> Redacted Text
[onSuccess] :: Request command -> Response -> command
[onError] :: Request command -> Text -> command

-- | Transport and tuning configuration for an Azure AI chat-completion
--   request.
data Config
Config :: Maybe Float -> Maybe Int -> Maybe Float -> Maybe Float -> Maybe Float -> AzureEndpoint -> Text -> Int -> Config

-- | Sampling temperature (omitted from the body when Nothing)
[temperature] :: Config -> Maybe Float

-- | Max tokens to generate (omitted from the body when Nothing)
[maxTokens] :: Config -> Maybe Int

-- | Nucleus sampling (omitted from the body when Nothing)
[topP] :: Config -> Maybe Float

-- | Frequency penalty (omitted from the body when Nothing)
[frequencyPenalty] :: Config -> Maybe Float

-- | Presence penalty (omitted from the body when Nothing)
[presencePenalty] :: Config -> Maybe Float

-- | Validated Azure resource endpoint (seeded by chatCompletion)
[endpoint] :: Config -> AzureEndpoint

-- | API version, e.g. "2024-10-21"
[apiVersion] :: Config -> Text

-- | Request timeout in seconds; default 60
[timeoutSeconds] :: Config -> Int

-- | Tuning + transport defaults: all sampling knobs Nothing, pinned
--   apiVersion, 60s timeout. The <a>endpoint</a> field is seeded with a
--   module-private placeholder that <a>chatCompletion</a> ALWAYS
--   overwrites (see note in the architecture doc).
--   
--   <pre>
--   AzureAI.defaultConfig { temperature = Just 0.5 }
--   </pre>
defaultConfig :: Config

-- | Read the (non-secret) URL out of a validated endpoint. This is a plain
--   newtype projection, NOT a secret unwrap — the https+allowlist
--   guarantee holds by construction, so reading the string is safe.
--   Exported for Internal.hs and tests; NOT re-exported from the facade.
endpointUrl :: AzureEndpoint -> Text

-- | Extract the host from an https URL, stripping scheme, path, query, and
--   fragment. SEC-002: strips <tt>?</tt> and <tt>#</tt> before the suffix
--   match so query/fragment-embedded allowed suffixes cannot smuggle a
--   host past <tt>endsWith</tt>.
hostOf :: Text -> Result Text Text
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Integration.AzureAI.Request.AzureEndpoint
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Integration.AzureAI.Request.Config
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Integration.AzureAI.Request.Config
instance GHC.Show.Show Integration.AzureAI.Request.AzureEndpoint
instance GHC.Show.Show Integration.AzureAI.Request.Config


-- | Internal implementation for Azure AI chat-completions integration.
--   
--   Contains the transformation from <a>Request</a> to <a>Request</a>, the
--   single <a>unwrap</a> site for the API key, and the 'ToAction (Request
--   command)' instance that enables <a>outbound</a>.
--   
--   <b>This module is not re-exported from <a>AzureAI</a></b> (only
--   <a>toHttpRequest</a> is re-exported from the facade as an advanced
--   escape hatch).

module Integration.AzureAI.Internal

-- | Bridge a Request into an Http.Request. The ONLY Redacted.unwrap site.
--   The endpoint is already https+allowlisted by construction
--   (AzureEndpoint), so there is no runtime https branch here — that guard
--   moved to azureEndpoint.
--   
--   <pre>
--   case AzureAI.azureEndpoint "<a>https://my-res.openai.azure.com"</a> of
--     Result.Ok endpoint -&gt;
--       AzureAI.chatCompletion endpoint msgs "gpt-4o" onOk onErr
--         |&gt; AzureAI.toHttpRequest
--         |&gt; Integration.outbound
--     Result.Err reason -&gt; Integration.none
--   </pre>
toHttpRequest :: Request command -> Request command

-- | Internal wire-format JSON body, not exposed to Integration.AzureAI
--   callers. Mirrors <a>RequestBody</a> minus tools/tool_choice.
data RequestBody
RequestBody :: Array Message -> Text -> Bool -> Maybe Float -> Maybe Int -> Maybe Float -> Maybe Float -> Maybe Float -> RequestBody
[messages] :: RequestBody -> Array Message
[model] :: RequestBody -> Text
[stream] :: RequestBody -> Bool
[temperature] :: RequestBody -> Maybe Float
[max_tokens] :: RequestBody -> Maybe Int
[top_p] :: RequestBody -> Maybe Float
[frequency_penalty] :: RequestBody -> Maybe Float
[presence_penalty] :: RequestBody -> Maybe Float

-- | Build the JSON request body from a Request. Call sites: (1)
--   toHttpRequest (production), (2) InternalSpec body-shape tests.
buildRequestBody :: Request command -> RequestBody

-- | Neutralise query-parameter injection through apiVersion (SEC-003).
--   Keeps only [A-Za-z0-9-]; drops <tt>&amp;</tt>, <tt>?</tt>, <tt>#</tt>,
--   whitespace, and all else. Legitimate values ("2024-10-21",
--   "2024-05-01-preview") pass through unchanged. Call sites: (1)
--   toHttpRequest (production), (2) InternalSpec SEC-003 tests.
sanitizeApiVersion :: Text -> Text

-- | Dispatch the Model Inference HTTP response on status code. 2xx: decode
--   as Response and call onSuccess; errors: sanitised onError (no key, no
--   raw body). Mirrors OpenRouter.Internal.handleSuccess with
--   Azure-branded messages. Call sites: (1) toHttpRequest (as
--   Http.Request.onSuccess), (2) InternalSpec.
handleSuccess :: Request command -> Response -> command

-- | Pass a transport-level error through to onError unchanged.
--   Integration.Http already sanitises transport errors. Call sites: (1)
--   toHttpRequest (as Http.Request.onError), (2) InternalSpec.
handleError :: Request command -> Text -> command
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Integration.AzureAI.Internal.RequestBody
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Integration.AzureAI.Internal.RequestBody
instance GHC.Show.Show Integration.AzureAI.Internal.RequestBody
instance (Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON command, GHC.TypeLits.KnownSymbol (Service.Command.Core.NameOf command)) => Integration.ToAction (Integration.AzureAI.Request.Request command)
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Integration.AzureAI.Internal.RequestBody


module Integration.AzureAI

-- | A validated Azure resource endpoint (https:// + allowed host suffix).
--   The constructor is NOT exported: use <a>azureEndpoint</a> or
--   <a>azureEndpointAllowing</a>. SEC-001: closed by construction — no
--   external caller can forge a host.
data AzureEndpoint

-- | Build an endpoint validated against the DEFAULT Azure host suffixes.
--   Delegates to <a>azureEndpointAllowing</a> with no extra suffixes.
--   
--   <pre>
--   case AzureAI.azureEndpoint "<a>https://my-res.openai.azure.com"</a> of
--     Result.Ok endpoint -&gt; useEndpoint endpoint
--     Result.Err reason  -&gt; logRejected reason
--   </pre>
azureEndpoint :: Text -> Result Text AzureEndpoint

-- | Same validation, plus caller-supplied ADDITIONAL host suffixes for
--   APIM custom domains / Private Endpoints. The single Result-returning
--   construction path. SEC-001: https scheme enforced; host must end with
--   an allowlisted suffix. SEC-002: <a>hostOf</a> strips query/fragment
--   before the suffix match.
--   
--   <pre>
--   AzureAI.azureEndpointAllowing
--     ["ai.contoso-internal.net"]
--     "<a>https://gateway.ai.contoso-internal.net"</a>
--   </pre>
azureEndpointAllowing :: Array Text -> Text -> Result Text AzureEndpoint

-- | Default allowed host suffixes: public Azure AI hosts plus sovereign
--   clouds.
--   
--   <pre>
--   -- openai.azure.com, cognitiveservices.azure.com, services.ai.azure.com,
--   -- inference.ai.azure.com, azure.us (Government), azure.cn (21Vianet)
--   AzureAI.defaultAzureHostSuffixes
--   </pre>
defaultAzureHostSuffixes :: Array Text

-- | Primary smart constructor. Sources the API key from
--   ?config.azureAiApiKey; seeds Config.endpoint from the validated
--   AzureEndpoint argument; apiVersion + tuning come from defaultConfig
--   (override via the Request/Config record).
--   
--   <pre>
--   case AzureAI.azureEndpoint "<a>https://my-res.openai.azure.com"</a> of
--     Result.Ok endpoint -&gt;
--       AzureAI.chatCompletion
--         endpoint
--         [AzureAI.system "Be concise.", AzureAI.user question]
--         "gpt-4o"
--         (\response -&gt; GotAnswer { response })
--         (\err -&gt; AiError { err })
--         |&gt; Integration.outbound
--     Result.Err reason -&gt;
--       Integration.none
--   </pre>
chatCompletion :: forall command config. (?config :: config, HasField "azureAiApiKey" config (Redacted Text)) => AzureEndpoint -> Array Message -> Text -> (Response -> command) -> (Text -> command) -> Request command

-- | The request the caller builds; consumed by Integration.outbound.
--   <a>apiKey</a> is 'Redacted Text', sourced from ?config by
--   <a>chatCompletion</a>. No Show/ToJSON — the unwrapped key can never
--   leak via a derived instance.
data Request command
Request :: Array Message -> Text -> Config -> Redacted Text -> (Response -> command) -> (Text -> command) -> Request command
[messages] :: Request command -> Array Message
[model] :: Request command -> Text
[config] :: Request command -> Config
[apiKey] :: Request command -> Redacted Text
[onSuccess] :: Request command -> Response -> command
[onError] :: Request command -> Text -> command

-- | Transport and tuning configuration for an Azure AI chat-completion
--   request.
data Config
Config :: Maybe Float -> Maybe Int -> Maybe Float -> Maybe Float -> Maybe Float -> AzureEndpoint -> Text -> Int -> Config

-- | Sampling temperature (omitted from the body when Nothing)
[temperature] :: Config -> Maybe Float

-- | Max tokens to generate (omitted from the body when Nothing)
[maxTokens] :: Config -> Maybe Int

-- | Nucleus sampling (omitted from the body when Nothing)
[topP] :: Config -> Maybe Float

-- | Frequency penalty (omitted from the body when Nothing)
[frequencyPenalty] :: Config -> Maybe Float

-- | Presence penalty (omitted from the body when Nothing)
[presencePenalty] :: Config -> Maybe Float

-- | Validated Azure resource endpoint (seeded by chatCompletion)
[endpoint] :: Config -> AzureEndpoint

-- | API version, e.g. "2024-10-21"
[apiVersion] :: Config -> Text

-- | Request timeout in seconds; default 60
[timeoutSeconds] :: Config -> Int

-- | Tuning + transport defaults: all sampling knobs Nothing, pinned
--   apiVersion, 60s timeout. The <a>endpoint</a> field is seeded with a
--   module-private placeholder that <a>chatCompletion</a> ALWAYS
--   overwrites (see note in the architecture doc).
--   
--   <pre>
--   AzureAI.defaultConfig { temperature = Just 0.5 }
--   </pre>
defaultConfig :: Config

-- | Bridge a Request into an Http.Request. The ONLY Redacted.unwrap site.
--   The endpoint is already https+allowlisted by construction
--   (AzureEndpoint), so there is no runtime https branch here — that guard
--   moved to azureEndpoint.
--   
--   <pre>
--   case AzureAI.azureEndpoint "<a>https://my-res.openai.azure.com"</a> of
--     Result.Ok endpoint -&gt;
--       AzureAI.chatCompletion endpoint msgs "gpt-4o" onOk onErr
--         |&gt; AzureAI.toHttpRequest
--         |&gt; Integration.outbound
--     Result.Err reason -&gt; Integration.none
--   </pre>
toHttpRequest :: Request command -> Request command

-- | Full response from OpenRouter chat completion API.
--   
--   This is what you receive in your <tt>onSuccess</tt> callback:
--   
--   <pre>
--   onSuccess = \response -&gt;
--     case response.choices |&gt; Array.first of
--       Just choice -&gt; GotAnswer { answer = choice.message.content }
--       Nothing -&gt; NoAnswer
--   </pre>
data Response
Response :: Text -> Text -> Array Choice -> Maybe Usage -> Response

-- | Unique generation identifier (e.g., "gen-abc123")
[id] :: Response -> Text

-- | The model that processed the request
[model] :: Response -> Text

-- | Completion choices (usually one element)
[choices] :: Response -> Array Choice

-- | Token usage statistics (may be absent)
[usage] :: Response -> Maybe Usage

-- | A single completion choice from the model.
--   
--   Most requests return a single choice. <a>toolCalls</a> is populated
--   for tool_choice = "required" requests, empty for regular chat
--   completion requests (backward compatible).
data Choice
Choice :: Message -> FinishReason -> {-# UNPACK #-}Int -> Array ToolCall -> Choice

-- | The assistant's response message
[message] :: Choice -> Message

-- | Why generation stopped
[finishReason] :: Choice -> FinishReason

-- | Index of this choice (0 for single responses)
[index] :: Choice -> {-# UNPACK #-}Int

-- | Tool calls made by the model (empty array for non-agent responses,
--   populated for tool_choice = "required" requests)
[toolCalls] :: Choice -> Array ToolCall

-- | Token usage statistics for the request.
--   
--   Useful for monitoring costs and optimizing prompts.
--   
--   <pre>
--   case response.usage of
--     Just usage -&gt; logUsage usage.totalTokens
--     Nothing -&gt; pure ()
--   </pre>
data Usage
Usage :: Int -> Int -> Int -> Usage

-- | Tokens used by the input messages
[promptTokens] :: Usage -> Int

-- | Tokens generated in the response
[completionTokens] :: Usage -> Int

-- | Total tokens (prompt + completion)
[totalTokens] :: Usage -> Int

-- | Why the model stopped generating tokens.
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><a>Stop</a> - Natural completion (hit stop sequence or end of
--   response)</li>
--   <li><a>Length</a> - Hit max_tokens limit</li>
--   <li><a>ContentFilter</a> - Content was filtered for safety</li>
--   <li><a>Unknown</a> - Provider returned an unrecognized reason</li>
--   </ul>
data FinishReason
Stop :: FinishReason
Length :: FinishReason
ContentFilter :: FinishReason
Unknown :: Text -> FinishReason

-- | A single message in a conversation.
--   
--   Messages form the conversation history sent to the model. Use the
--   smart constructors <a>user</a>, <a>assistant</a>, and <a>system</a>
--   for convenient message creation.
--   
--   The <a>content</a> field supports both plain text and multimodal
--   content. Text-only messages serialize identically to the previous
--   format.
data Message
Message :: Role -> Content -> Message
[role] :: Message -> Role
[content] :: Message -> Content

-- | Role of a message participant in the conversation.
--   
--   OpenRouter (like OpenAI) uses roles to distinguish between:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><a>User</a> - The human asking questions</li>
--   <li><a>Assistant</a> - The AI responding</li>
--   <li><a>System</a> - Instructions that guide the AI's behavior</li>
--   </ul>
data Role
User :: Role
Assistant :: Role
System :: Role

-- | Message content — either plain text or multimodal content array.
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><a>TextContent</a> — Plain text (backward compatible with previous
--   API)</li>
--   <li><a>MultiContent</a> — Array of content parts for multimodal
--   messages</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   <h2>JSON Serialization</h2>
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><tt>TextContent "hello"</tt> → <tt>"hello"</tt> (plain JSON
--   string)</li>
--   <li><tt>MultiContent [...]</tt> → <tt>[{"type":"text",...}, ...]</tt>
--   (JSON array)</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   <tt>FromJSON</tt> accepts both formats for backward compatibility.
data Content

-- | Plain text content (backward compatible)
TextContent :: Text -> Content

-- | Array of content parts (text + images/attachments)
MultiContent :: Array ContentPart -> Content

-- | A single part within multimodal content.
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><a>TextPart</a> — A text segment</li>
--   <li><a>ImageUrlPart</a> — An image or document attachment</li>
--   </ul>
data ContentPart

-- | Text content part
TextPart :: Text -> ContentPart

-- | Image/attachment content part
ImageUrlPart :: ImageUrl -> ContentPart

-- | Image URL reference for multimodal content.
--   
--   Used to embed images or documents as data URLs:
--   
--   <pre>
--   ImageUrl { url = "data:application/pdf;base64,..." }
--   </pre>
data ImageUrl
ImageUrl :: Text -> ImageUrl

-- | Data URL (e.g., <tt>"data:application/pdf;base64,..."</tt>)
[url] :: ImageUrl -> Text

-- | Create a user message.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Message.user "What is the meaning of life?"
--   </pre>
user :: Text -> Message

-- | Create an assistant message.
--   
--   Useful for providing example responses or continuing a conversation:
--   
--   <pre>
--   Message.assistant "I'd be happy to help with that."
--   </pre>
assistant :: Text -> Message

-- | Create a system message.
--   
--   System messages set the AI's behavior and personality:
--   
--   <pre>
--   Message.system "You are a helpful coding assistant. Be concise."
--   </pre>
system :: Text -> Message

-- | Create a multimodal user message with an attachment.
--   
--   Builds a message with both a text prompt and an image/document
--   attachment encoded as a data URL.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Message.userWithAttachment
--     "Extract all text from this document."
--     base64Content
--     "application/pdf"
--   </pre>
userWithAttachment :: Text -> Text -> Text -> Message


module Integration.Audio.Transcribe.Internal
buildSystemPrompt :: Config -> Text
buildTranscriptionPrompt :: Config -> Text
fileAccessToIntegrationError :: FileAccessError -> IntegrationError
integrationErrorToText :: IntegrationError -> Text
instance (Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON command, GHC.TypeLits.KnownSymbol (Service.Command.Core.NameOf command)) => Integration.ToAction (Integration.Audio.Transcribe.Request command)


module Integration.Agent.Internal
parseFirstToolCall :: Response -> Result Text ToolCall
validateToolName :: Array CommandTool -> ToolCall -> Result Text ToolCall
decodeArguments :: FromJSON command => ToolCall -> Result Text command
buildMessages :: Config -> Text -> Array Message
buildAgentRequestBody :: Request command -> RequestBody
handleAgentHttpSuccess :: FromJSON command => Request command -> Response -> command
handleAgentHttpError :: Request command -> Text -> command
executeAgent :: (ToJSON command, FromJSON command, KnownSymbol (NameOf command)) => ActionContext -> Request command -> Task IntegrationError (Maybe CommandPayload)
openRouterBaseUrl :: Text
instance (Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON command, Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON command, GHC.TypeLits.KnownSymbol (Service.Command.Core.NameOf command)) => Integration.ToAction (Integration.Agent.Types.Request command)


-- | OAuth2 provider configuration for Oura Ring API integration.
--   
--   This module provides the OAuth2 configuration needed to enable users
--   to connect their Oura accounts via the `<i>connect</i>oura` endpoint.
--   
--   <h1>Oura OAuth2 Scopes (v2 API)</h1>
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><tt>email</tt>: User email address</li>
--   <li><tt>personal</tt>: Personal info (age, weight, height, biological
--   sex)</li>
--   <li><tt>daily</tt>: Daily summaries (sleep, activity, readiness,
--   cardiovascular age, resilience, stress, sleep_time)</li>
--   <li><tt>heartrate</tt>: Heart rate data</li>
--   <li><tt>workout</tt>: Workout data</li>
--   <li><tt>tag</tt>: Enhanced tag data</li>
--   <li><tt>session</tt>: Session data</li>
--   <li><tt>spo2</tt>: SpO2 readings</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   See:
--   <a>https://cloud.ouraring.com/docs/authentication#oauth2-scopes</a>

module Integration.Oura.Provider

-- | Oura Ring OAuth2 provider configuration.
--   
--   Contains the endpoints needed for the Authorization Code flow.
ouraProvider :: Provider

-- | Create an OAuth2ProviderConfig for Oura Ring integration.
--   
--   This function constructs the complete OAuth2 configuration needed to
--   handle the OAuth2 flow for Oura Ring API connections.
--   
--   <h1>Parameters</h1>
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><tt>clientId</tt>: OAuth2 client ID from Oura dashboard</li>
--   <li><tt>clientSecret</tt>: OAuth2 client secret from Oura dashboard
--   (keep secure!)</li>
--   <li><tt>redirectUri</tt>: Callback URL (must match Oura dashboard
--   configuration)</li>
--   <li><tt>onSuccess</tt>: Callback when token exchange succeeds (userId
--   -&gt; tokenKey -&gt; action JSON)</li>
--   <li><tt>onFailure</tt>: Callback when OAuth2 flow fails (userId -&gt;
--   error -&gt; action JSON)</li>
--   <li><tt>onDisconnect</tt>: Callback when user disconnects the provider
--   (userId -&gt; action JSON)</li>
--   <li><tt>successRedirectUrl</tt>: URL to redirect user after successful
--   connection</li>
--   <li><tt>failureRedirectUrl</tt>: URL to redirect user after failed
--   connection</li>
--   </ul>
makeOuraConfig :: ClientId -> ClientSecret -> RedirectUri -> (Text -> TokenKey -> Text) -> (Text -> OAuth2Error -> Text) -> (Text -> Text) -> Text -> Text -> OAuth2ProviderConfig


module Integration.Oura.Types

-- | Wrapper for paginated API responses
data PaginatedResponse value
PaginatedResponse :: [value] -> Maybe Text -> PaginatedResponse value
[data_] :: PaginatedResponse value -> [value]
[nextToken] :: PaginatedResponse value -> Maybe Text

-- | Daily sleep data from Oura Ring
data SleepData
SleepData :: Text -> Text -> Maybe Int -> Text -> Maybe SleepContributors -> SleepData
[id] :: SleepData -> Text
[day] :: SleepData -> Text
[score] :: SleepData -> Maybe Int
[timestamp] :: SleepData -> Text
[contributors] :: SleepData -> Maybe SleepContributors

-- | Contributors to sleep score
data SleepContributors
SleepContributors :: Maybe Int -> Maybe Int -> Maybe Int -> Maybe Int -> Maybe Int -> Maybe Int -> Maybe Int -> SleepContributors
[deepSleep] :: SleepContributors -> Maybe Int
[efficiency] :: SleepContributors -> Maybe Int
[latency] :: SleepContributors -> Maybe Int
[remSleep] :: SleepContributors -> Maybe Int
[restfulness] :: SleepContributors -> Maybe Int
[timing] :: SleepContributors -> Maybe Int
[totalSleep] :: SleepContributors -> Maybe Int

-- | Daily activity data from Oura Ring
data ActivityData
ActivityData :: Text -> Text -> Maybe Int -> Maybe Int -> Maybe Int -> Maybe Int -> Maybe Int -> Maybe Int -> Maybe Int -> Text -> Maybe Float -> Maybe Text -> Maybe ActivityContributors -> Maybe Int -> Maybe Int -> Maybe Int -> Maybe Int -> Maybe Int -> Maybe SampleModel -> Maybe Int -> Maybe Int -> Maybe Int -> Maybe Int -> Maybe Int -> Maybe Int -> Maybe Int -> ActivityData
[id] :: ActivityData -> Text
[day] :: ActivityData -> Text
[score] :: ActivityData -> Maybe Int
[activeCalories] :: ActivityData -> Maybe Int
[steps] :: ActivityData -> Maybe Int
[totalCalories] :: ActivityData -> Maybe Int
[highActivityTime] :: ActivityData -> Maybe Int
[mediumActivityTime] :: ActivityData -> Maybe Int
[lowActivityTime] :: ActivityData -> Maybe Int
[timestamp] :: ActivityData -> Text
[averageMetMinutes] :: ActivityData -> Maybe Float
[class5Min] :: ActivityData -> Maybe Text
[contributors] :: ActivityData -> Maybe ActivityContributors
[equivalentWalkingDistance] :: ActivityData -> Maybe Int
[highActivityMetMinutes] :: ActivityData -> Maybe Int
[inactivityAlerts] :: ActivityData -> Maybe Int
[lowActivityMetMinutes] :: ActivityData -> Maybe Int
[mediumActivityMetMinutes] :: ActivityData -> Maybe Int
[met] :: ActivityData -> Maybe SampleModel
[metersToTarget] :: ActivityData -> Maybe Int
[nonWearTime] :: ActivityData -> Maybe Int
[restingTime] :: ActivityData -> Maybe Int
[sedentaryMetMinutes] :: ActivityData -> Maybe Int
[sedentaryTime] :: ActivityData -> Maybe Int
[targetCalories] :: ActivityData -> Maybe Int
[targetMeters] :: ActivityData -> Maybe Int

-- | Daily readiness data from Oura Ring
data ReadinessData
ReadinessData :: Text -> Text -> Maybe Int -> Maybe Float -> Maybe Float -> Text -> Maybe ReadinessContributors -> ReadinessData
[id] :: ReadinessData -> Text
[day] :: ReadinessData -> Text
[score] :: ReadinessData -> Maybe Int
[temperatureDeviation] :: ReadinessData -> Maybe Float
[temperatureTrendDeviation] :: ReadinessData -> Maybe Float
[timestamp] :: ReadinessData -> Text
[contributors] :: ReadinessData -> Maybe ReadinessContributors

-- | Contributors to readiness score
data ReadinessContributors
ReadinessContributors :: Maybe Int -> Maybe Int -> Maybe Int -> Maybe Int -> Maybe Int -> Maybe Int -> Maybe Int -> Maybe Int -> Maybe Int -> ReadinessContributors
[activityBalance] :: ReadinessContributors -> Maybe Int
[bodyTemperature] :: ReadinessContributors -> Maybe Int
[hrvBalance] :: ReadinessContributors -> Maybe Int
[previousDayActivity] :: ReadinessContributors -> Maybe Int
[previousNight] :: ReadinessContributors -> Maybe Int
[recoveryIndex] :: ReadinessContributors -> Maybe Int
[restingHeartRate] :: ReadinessContributors -> Maybe Int
[sleepBalance] :: ReadinessContributors -> Maybe Int
[sleepRegularity] :: ReadinessContributors -> Maybe Int

-- | Heart rate data from Oura Ring
data HeartRateData
HeartRateData :: Int -> Text -> Text -> HeartRateData
[bpm] :: HeartRateData -> Int
[source] :: HeartRateData -> Text
[timestamp] :: HeartRateData -> Text

-- | Time-series data with sampling interval and items
data SampleModel
SampleModel :: Maybe Float -> Maybe [Maybe Float] -> Maybe Text -> SampleModel
[interval] :: SampleModel -> Maybe Float
[items] :: SampleModel -> Maybe [Maybe Float]
[timestamp] :: SampleModel -> Maybe Text

-- | Contributors to activity score
data ActivityContributors
ActivityContributors :: Maybe Int -> Maybe Int -> Maybe Int -> Maybe Int -> Maybe Int -> Maybe Int -> ActivityContributors
[meetDailyTargets] :: ActivityContributors -> Maybe Int
[moveEveryHour] :: ActivityContributors -> Maybe Int
[recoveryTime] :: ActivityContributors -> Maybe Int
[stayActive] :: ActivityContributors -> Maybe Int
[trainingFrequency] :: ActivityContributors -> Maybe Int
[trainingVolume] :: ActivityContributors -> Maybe Int

-- | Contributors to resilience score
data ResilienceContributors
ResilienceContributors :: Maybe Float -> Maybe Float -> Maybe Float -> ResilienceContributors
[sleepRecovery] :: ResilienceContributors -> Maybe Float
[daytimeRecovery] :: ResilienceContributors -> Maybe Float
[stress] :: ResilienceContributors -> Maybe Float

-- | Aggregated SpO2 values
data SpO2AggregatedValues
SpO2AggregatedValues :: Float -> SpO2AggregatedValues
[average] :: SpO2AggregatedValues -> Float

-- | Rest mode episode
data RestModeEpisode
RestModeEpisode :: Maybe [Text] -> Text -> RestModeEpisode
[tags] :: RestModeEpisode -> Maybe [Text]
[timestamp] :: RestModeEpisode -> Text

-- | Sleep period readiness data
data SleepPeriodReadiness
SleepPeriodReadiness :: Maybe ReadinessContributors -> Maybe Int -> Maybe Float -> Maybe Float -> SleepPeriodReadiness
[contributors] :: SleepPeriodReadiness -> Maybe ReadinessContributors
[score] :: SleepPeriodReadiness -> Maybe Int
[temperatureDeviation] :: SleepPeriodReadiness -> Maybe Float
[temperatureTrendDeviation] :: SleepPeriodReadiness -> Maybe Float

-- | Detailed sleep period data from Oura Ring
data SleepPeriodData
SleepPeriodData :: Text -> Text -> Text -> Maybe Float -> Maybe Float -> Maybe Float -> Maybe Int -> Maybe Text -> Maybe Text -> Maybe Int -> Maybe Int -> Maybe SampleModel -> Maybe SampleModel -> Maybe Int -> Maybe Int -> Maybe Bool -> Maybe Int -> Maybe Text -> Maybe Int -> Maybe SleepPeriodReadiness -> Maybe Float -> Maybe Int -> Maybe Int -> Maybe Text -> Maybe Text -> Maybe Float -> Maybe Int -> Maybe Int -> Maybe Text -> SleepPeriodData
[id] :: SleepPeriodData -> Text
[day] :: SleepPeriodData -> Text
[timestamp] :: SleepPeriodData -> Text
[averageBreath] :: SleepPeriodData -> Maybe Float
[averageHeartRate] :: SleepPeriodData -> Maybe Float
[averageHrv] :: SleepPeriodData -> Maybe Float
[awakeTime] :: SleepPeriodData -> Maybe Int
[bedtimeEnd] :: SleepPeriodData -> Maybe Text
[bedtimeStart] :: SleepPeriodData -> Maybe Text
[deepSleepDuration] :: SleepPeriodData -> Maybe Int
[efficiency] :: SleepPeriodData -> Maybe Int
[heartRate] :: SleepPeriodData -> Maybe SampleModel
[hrv] :: SleepPeriodData -> Maybe SampleModel
[latency] :: SleepPeriodData -> Maybe Int
[lightSleepDuration] :: SleepPeriodData -> Maybe Int
[lowBatteryAlert] :: SleepPeriodData -> Maybe Bool
[lowestHeartRate] :: SleepPeriodData -> Maybe Int
[movementThirtySec] :: SleepPeriodData -> Maybe Text
[period] :: SleepPeriodData -> Maybe Int
[readiness] :: SleepPeriodData -> Maybe SleepPeriodReadiness
[readinessScoreDelta] :: SleepPeriodData -> Maybe Float
[remSleepDuration] :: SleepPeriodData -> Maybe Int
[restlessPeriods] :: SleepPeriodData -> Maybe Int
[sleepAlgorithmVersion] :: SleepPeriodData -> Maybe Text
[sleepPhase5Min] :: SleepPeriodData -> Maybe Text
[sleepScoreDelta] :: SleepPeriodData -> Maybe Float
[timeInBed] :: SleepPeriodData -> Maybe Int
[totalSleepDuration] :: SleepPeriodData -> Maybe Int
[type_] :: SleepPeriodData -> Maybe Text

-- | Workout data from Oura Ring
data WorkoutData
WorkoutData :: Text -> Text -> Maybe Text -> Maybe Float -> Maybe Float -> Maybe Text -> Maybe Text -> Maybe Text -> Maybe Text -> Maybe Text -> WorkoutData
[id] :: WorkoutData -> Text
[day] :: WorkoutData -> Text
[activity] :: WorkoutData -> Maybe Text
[calories] :: WorkoutData -> Maybe Float
[distance] :: WorkoutData -> Maybe Float
[endDatetime] :: WorkoutData -> Maybe Text
[intensity] :: WorkoutData -> Maybe Text
[label] :: WorkoutData -> Maybe Text
[source] :: WorkoutData -> Maybe Text
[startDatetime] :: WorkoutData -> Maybe Text

-- | Session data from Oura Ring
data SessionData
SessionData :: Text -> Text -> Maybe Text -> Maybe SampleModel -> Maybe SampleModel -> Maybe Text -> Maybe SampleModel -> Maybe Text -> Maybe Text -> SessionData
[id] :: SessionData -> Text
[day] :: SessionData -> Text
[endDatetime] :: SessionData -> Maybe Text
[heartRate] :: SessionData -> Maybe SampleModel
[hrv] :: SessionData -> Maybe SampleModel
[mood] :: SessionData -> Maybe Text
[motionCount] :: SessionData -> Maybe SampleModel
[startDatetime] :: SessionData -> Maybe Text
[type_] :: SessionData -> Maybe Text

-- | Personal info data from Oura Ring
data PersonalInfoData
PersonalInfoData :: Text -> Maybe Int -> Maybe Float -> Maybe Float -> Maybe Text -> Maybe Text -> PersonalInfoData
[id] :: PersonalInfoData -> Text
[age] :: PersonalInfoData -> Maybe Int
[weight] :: PersonalInfoData -> Maybe Float
[height] :: PersonalInfoData -> Maybe Float
[biologicalSex] :: PersonalInfoData -> Maybe Text
[email] :: PersonalInfoData -> Maybe Text

-- | Daily stress data from Oura Ring
data DailyStressData
DailyStressData :: Text -> Text -> Maybe Float -> Maybe Float -> Maybe Text -> DailyStressData
[id] :: DailyStressData -> Text
[day] :: DailyStressData -> Text
[stressHigh] :: DailyStressData -> Maybe Float
[recoveryHigh] :: DailyStressData -> Maybe Float
[daySummary] :: DailyStressData -> Maybe Text

-- | Daily SpO2 data from Oura Ring
data DailySpO2Data
DailySpO2Data :: Text -> Text -> Maybe SpO2AggregatedValues -> DailySpO2Data
[id] :: DailySpO2Data -> Text
[day] :: DailySpO2Data -> Text
[spO2Percentage] :: DailySpO2Data -> Maybe SpO2AggregatedValues

-- | Daily resilience data from Oura Ring
data DailyResilienceData
DailyResilienceData :: Text -> Text -> Maybe ResilienceContributors -> Maybe Text -> DailyResilienceData
[id] :: DailyResilienceData -> Text
[day] :: DailyResilienceData -> Text
[contributors] :: DailyResilienceData -> Maybe ResilienceContributors
[level] :: DailyResilienceData -> Maybe Text

-- | Daily cardiovascular age data from Oura Ring
data DailyCardiovascularAgeData
DailyCardiovascularAgeData :: Text -> Text -> Maybe Int -> DailyCardiovascularAgeData
[id] :: DailyCardiovascularAgeData -> Text
[day] :: DailyCardiovascularAgeData -> Text
[vascularAge] :: DailyCardiovascularAgeData -> Maybe Int

-- | VO2 max data from Oura Ring
data VO2MaxData
VO2MaxData :: Text -> Text -> Maybe Float -> Maybe Text -> VO2MaxData
[id] :: VO2MaxData -> Text
[day] :: VO2MaxData -> Text
[vo2Max] :: VO2MaxData -> Maybe Float
[timestamp] :: VO2MaxData -> Maybe Text

-- | Enhanced tag data from Oura Ring
data EnhancedTagData
EnhancedTagData :: Text -> Text -> Maybe Text -> Maybe Text -> Maybe Text -> Maybe Text -> Maybe Text -> Maybe Text -> Maybe Text -> EnhancedTagData
[id] :: EnhancedTagData -> Text
[day] :: EnhancedTagData -> Text
[tagTypeCode] :: EnhancedTagData -> Maybe Text
[startTime] :: EnhancedTagData -> Maybe Text
[endTime] :: EnhancedTagData -> Maybe Text
[startDatetime] :: EnhancedTagData -> Maybe Text
[endDatetime] :: EnhancedTagData -> Maybe Text
[comment] :: EnhancedTagData -> Maybe Text
[timestamp] :: EnhancedTagData -> Maybe Text

-- | Sleep time recommendation data from Oura Ring
data SleepTimeData
SleepTimeData :: Text -> Text -> Maybe Text -> Maybe Text -> Maybe Text -> Maybe Text -> Maybe Text -> SleepTimeData
[id] :: SleepTimeData -> Text
[day] :: SleepTimeData -> Text
[optimalBedtimeStart] :: SleepTimeData -> Maybe Text
[optimalBedtimeEnd] :: SleepTimeData -> Maybe Text
[recommendation] :: SleepTimeData -> Maybe Text
[status] :: SleepTimeData -> Maybe Text
[timestamp] :: SleepTimeData -> Maybe Text

-- | Rest mode period data from Oura Ring
data RestModePeriodData
RestModePeriodData :: Text -> Maybe Text -> Maybe Text -> Maybe [RestModeEpisode] -> Maybe Text -> Maybe Text -> RestModePeriodData
[id] :: RestModePeriodData -> Text
[endDate] :: RestModePeriodData -> Maybe Text
[endDay] :: RestModePeriodData -> Maybe Text
[episodes] :: RestModePeriodData -> Maybe [RestModeEpisode]
[startDate] :: RestModePeriodData -> Maybe Text
[startDay] :: RestModePeriodData -> Maybe Text

-- | Ring configuration data from Oura Ring
data RingConfigurationData
RingConfigurationData :: Text -> Maybe Text -> Maybe Text -> Maybe Text -> Maybe Text -> Maybe Text -> Maybe Int -> RingConfigurationData
[id] :: RingConfigurationData -> Text
[color] :: RingConfigurationData -> Maybe Text
[design] :: RingConfigurationData -> Maybe Text
[firmwareVersion] :: RingConfigurationData -> Maybe Text
[hardwareType] :: RingConfigurationData -> Maybe Text
[setUpAt] :: RingConfigurationData -> Maybe Text
[size] :: RingConfigurationData -> Maybe Int
